Latest community news Charity Super.Mkt is heading to the Arndale – next weekend . . Latest . community news . Charity Super.Mkt is heading to the Arndale – next weekend. By. About Manchester - . . July 2, 2026 0 . . . . . . Facebook. . Twitter. . Google+. . Pinterest. . WhatsApp. . . . . . Screenshot . Charity Super.Mkt, the UK’s first multi-charity fashion store, is set to make its Central Manchester debut when it opens its doors in Exchange Court at Manchester Arndale this July. The pop-up will run from 10th–12th July, bringing three days of curated second-hand fashion to one of the city’s most iconic retail destinations. The event marks a significant milestone for the concept – its first retail-centred pop-up in Central Manchester.. Shoppers can expect a vibrant, carefully curated edit of pre-loved clothing sourced from both national and local charities. From standout vintage pieces and coveted band tees to forgotten football tops, desirable denim, and everything in between. Participating charities include: The British Heart Foundation and the RSPCA.. Having previously wowed shoppers during a highly successful two-month residency at MediaCityUK in October 2023, Charity Super.Mkt arrives in the city centre riding a wave of momentum and long-awaited return. The MediaCityUK pop-up exceeded all expectations: more than 10,000 items were sold, over 3 tonnes of textile waste were diverted from landfill, more than 25 tonnes of CO₂ emissions were offset, and upwards of 4,500 cubic metres of water were saved.. Charity Super.Mkt is co-founded by Wayne Hemingway MBE, of HemingwayDesign and co-founder of British fashion brand Red or Dead, and Maria Chenoweth, CEO of sustainable clothing charity Traid. Together, they have created a destination that allows people to shop sustainably and with purpose – proving that doing good and dressing well are not mutually exclusive.. The Arndale pop-up carries a particular resonance for co-founder Wayne Hemingway MBE, whose connection to Manchester’s second-hand and fashion retail scene runs deep. Hemingway co-founded Red or Dead in 1982 alongside his wife Gerardine, some of the first outlets were in Afflecks Palace, The Royal Exchange and on King Street.. Red or Dead went on to shake up the British fashion industry with its anti-establishment approach to design and retail, winning the British Fashion Council’s Designer of the Year Award for three consecutive years, and planting firm roots in Manchester’s club culture landscape t

A Tameside army veteran has thanked local NHS mental health services for ‘life-changing’ support for post-traumatic stress disorder, panic disorder and anxiety Gary Hind, 66, from Hyde, was referred to NHS Tameside and Glossop Talking Therapies, run by Pennine Care NHS Foundation Trust, after his anxiety began to cause tightness in his chest, heart flutters and breathlessness The pain became so severe it left Gary physically floored and concerned for his health Gary had lived with mental health difficulties since childhood, following a troubled home situation and the sudden death of his father When he was 16, Gary joined the British armed forces, leading to a very successful career that truly ‘turned his life around’. In 1999, he received an MBE for his service Gary said: “Being in the army rebuilt me as a person. I thrived and learnt a lot of discipline and control. I felt like a new person.”

The annual Summer Reading Challenge is back which means that children and their families have the chance to get involved in this hugely popular event to encourage children to keep reading during the summer holidays Launching today, 1 July, as part of a series of three events across the week, more than a hundred eager Year 1 and 2 pupils at Wythenshawe Forum Library enjoyed a football-themed reading activity - Footy Fairy tales - to coincide with the World Cup. With live music, super songs a And “Read to the Beat” the overarching theme for the Summer Reading Challenge this year is all about connecting reading with music, rhythm and creativity This popular book challenge has run in Manchester Libraries for more than 20 years and encourages children aged 4 -12 years to keep reading during the summer holidays helping to activate their imaginations and prevent the dip in literacy over the long summer holidays This year Manchester Libraries will be promoting the challenge to schools across the city to encourage pupils to sign up and visit their local library to borrow books and complete the challenge, helping children feel ready to return to school in September as well as supporting Ye To date the programme has been a great success and there are high hopes that the numbers signing up this year will surpass 2025's challenge, which saw 4,079 children join the challenge, with 1,995 reading six or more books

title: New outdoor basketball court opens in Cringle Park as part of city-wide investment in community sport
summary: Manchester City Council, in partnership with Manchester Basketball and the National Governing Body Basketball England, has completed a major refurbishment of the outdoor basketball court at Cringle Park in Levenshulme
lead: Manchester City Council, in partnership with Manchester Basketball and the National Governing Body Basketball England, has completed a major refurbishment of the outdoor basketball court at Cringle Park in Levenshulme
practical_angle: Полиция или суд сообщили новое развитие по делу.
raw_evidence: Manchester City Council, in partnership with Manchester Basketball and the National Governing Body Basketball England, has completed a major refurbishment of the outdoor basketball court at Cringle Park in Levenshulme.

Since the beginning of the year, a major review has been underway around the city’s draft Strategy for bringing empty homes back into use to help meet demand for affordable homes across the city – while also improving communities by tackling empty homes that can blight an area Following a major public consultation, the Council is now acting on feedback to accelerate plans to bring as many long-term empty homes back into active use as possible – proposals that saw strong public backing from residents (83% of the respondents to the recent consultation ag Although the number of long-term empty homes in Manchester remains close to an historic low – as the Council has managed to reduce the number more than half since 2013 – every void property is a missed opportunity to provide a family a place to live, reduce homelessness and lesse Feedback from Manchester people has confirmed the impact in the city’s neighbourhoods. 86% of respondents to a recent consultation expressed concern about empty homes (51% very concerned) with the majority also reporting that they had seen empty homes fall into disrepair, become What to do if there is an empty home in your community An empty home is often easily recognisable on a street. The easiest way to report an empty home is to report the issue online or by emailing emptyhomesteam@manchester.gov.uk

A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families The property in Greenwood Road, Woodhouse Park, has been acquired by the Council from registered housing provider Mosscare St Vincent’s as part of wider plans to boost the city’s stock of publicly-owned, quality temporary accommodation Work is due to start this summer, and be completed in December this year, to refurbish the property as 17 self-contained flats for homeless families from Wythenshawe and south Manchester All flats will be refurbished, including new kitchens and bathrooms in each case. Two new laundry rooms for shared use will also be created, and gardens to the rear will have raised bedding for residents’ use The new accommodation will see families supported by an accommodation team based on site to help them move on as quickly as possible into permanent settled tenancies The Greenwood Road initiative is the latest example of the Council’s drive to increase its temporary accommodation stock across the city to reduce the number of out-of-area placements, helping people who become homeless to stay closer to their previous homes and support networks

Manchester Council Leader Bev Craig pays tribute to MOBO founder and trailblazer Kanya King CBE and her decades-long work championing black artists and bringing black music and culture to the mainstream here in the UK, following the announcement today of her passing

Two more men have been jailed for the murder of a 16-year-old student in Manchester, with the court hearing how one had also killed another man two days earlier through dangerous driving Shikeo Bell, 22, was sentenced to 18 years for helping murder Rhamero West following a car chase in the city in September 2021 He was one of a group of people who pursued the 16-year-old on foot before Rhamero was stabbed to death in broad daylight in the Old Trafford area Manchester Crown Court heard Bell is currently in prison for causing the death of a motorcyclist in Moston Giovanni Lawrence, 24, who drove one of the cars which chased Rhamero, was also sentenced to a minimum of 15 years for his part in the murder Lawrence was a driver of one of the stolen BMW's that chased Rhamero's car through Moss Side and Old Trafford, the court heard

The ringleader of a Rochdale grooming gang that targeted girls as young as 12 has been released from prison Shabir Ahmed, 73, who was known as 'Daddy' by his victims, was jailed for 22 years in August 2012 for a raft of child sexual offences including rape This week his victims were told he would be released on licence and despite earlier promises, could not be deported due to a 55-year-old law But Sir Keir Starmer has now asked the home secretary to review the case amid calls for the law to be changed to allow him to removed from the country The BBC understands Ahmed left prison earlier and is now in 24-hour staffed accommodation, wearing a GPS electronically monitored tag Some of his victims said they were "frightened" by his release, and they felt "unsafe"

A teenager who planned to fly to Somalia to join the Islamic State group has been jailed after he admitted planning a terrorist attack with a gun The Old Bailey in London heard how Italian national, Muhammad Billal, who lived in Nelson, Lancashire, had researched how to buy weapons and downloaded maps of locations including military bases in the UK and a college in his local area When police arrested Billal at Manchester Airport in November 2024 they found terrorist literature on his mobile phone, and an internet search history of guns, such as rifles and semi-automatic weaponry Billal, now 19, has been given a 13-and-a-half year sentence after pleading guilty to terrorism charges He was charged in February 2025 with two counts of preparation of terrorist acts and four counts of collecting information likely to be used by a person committing or preparing an act of terrorism The judge, Mrs Justice Cheema-Grubb, told Billal: "By your own account to the probation officer, you intended to kill members of the public and you hoped to be killed by police."

A former Stoke-on-Trent MP says she is optimistic about Andy Burnham's promise to provide "good growth to every postcode" if he becomes prime minister Joan Walley, who represented Stoke-on-Trent North between 1983 and 2015, told BBC Radio Stoke she got to know Burnham during his first period in parliament, between 2001 and 2017, when he was elected mayor of Greater Manchester "We're friends in the sense that our politics are very, very, very similar," Walley said But Burnham's approach has been criticised by some, including the chair of the Conservative Party, who feel it could increase bureaucracy and delay decision-making Walley's view though is that he has a "sense of urgency" in how he works - a view taken through a bond forged over a similarity in their constituencies - Burnham having been MP for Leigh, Greater Manchester It developed further when Walley and Burnham, along with Yorkshire MP Michael Clapham, were asked by late deputy Labour leader John Prescott to take part in a coal mining review, investigating issues in former coal mining areas

Manchester The vision for Manchester's future A blueprint for the city's future is being made by 2039 View 2 Images Manchester has gone through enormous changes in the last decades - but it's not over yet (Image: James Speakman/PA Wire) News Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 06:00, 03 Jul 2026 A report offers a glimpse of the major changes coming to Manchester over the next 15 years Bosses at the council are creating a blueprint for the city’s future, including expanding the city centre, building more homes, and cementing Manchester’s place as a top place to visit The vision has been shared in a report known as the local plan, covering the changes set to take place in the city between 2022 and 2039 It is at an ‘advanced and final stage’ and is being put before the council’s cabinet team at a public meeting next week "Manchester will be an even more must-see city, with amazing things to do," the report claims. "Our neighbourhoods will be clean and green, with good-quality affordable homes and good public services. Mancunians will feel safer indoors, outdoors and online."

Reddish 'It feels like Reddish is a cash cow, we pay lots in and get absolutely nothing in return' Stockport Council’s leadership said MP Navendu Mishra was wasting an opportunity just to ‘simply try and score political points’. Comments News Edward B People living in one Stockport area feel like it is a ‘cash cow’ where they ‘get absolutely nothing in return’, according to a local Labour MP. But Stockport council’s leadership said MP Navendu Mishra was wasting an opportunity just to ‘simply try and score political points’ There is currently an ongoing row over whether different parts of Stockport get more money than other areas which went to a Parliament debate on July 1. Politicians in opposition to the Liberal Democrat-led Stockport Council have recently criticised what they see as neglect by th Local authority leadership argue they have been continuing to fight for fair funding and grants at every level so the whole borough can benefit. Coun Dan Oliver previously said the claims were ‘irresponsible and completely untrue’ and pointed to the lack of government funding for While Mr Mishra argued during the July 1 debate both Labour and Conservative governments had not given enough funding to the borough, the Stockport MP said the council was making political choices to overlook Reddish amid concerns around ‘ageing infrastructure, a lack of high-qua The Stockport MP criticised the closed Stockport Central Library, the closure of the Harrison’s Weir bridge, parking charges, issues at the Lancashire Hill estate, a leaky roof at Reddish library, and the long-term closure of Reddish Baths, quoting one resident who said: “It feel

Greater Manchester Police Revealed: The shocking texts sent by a sex-obsessed police officer that saw him leave GMP in disgrace PC Michael Jones is the latest cop to leave Greater Manchester Police over contact with sex workers View 2 Images Greater M News John Scheerhout Crime reporter 06:00, 03 Jul 2026 A sex-obsessed police constable has left GMP in disgrace after he was caught messaging a series of women about his erotic fantasies while on duty - even managing to meet one of them for an illicit hour-long liaison minutes be PC Michael Jones bombarded his contacts with texts expressing interest in spanking in public and BDSM (bondage, dominance, submission and sadomasochism) and he had sex with two of them, a disciplinary panel found. One of these sexual encounters was in a car park Messages uncovered by an internal investigation revealed that on one occasion he discussed his sexual fantasy of playing 'daddy' while publicly spanking his 'princess' PC Jones resigned from GMP moments before a disciplinary hearing began and later concluded he was guilty of gross misconduct and would have been sacked had he not already left the force. The officer resigned after his bid to adjourn disciplinary proceedings was turned down The disciplinary hearing on June 11 this year heard shocking details about the messages he exchanged with contacts in his phone named only as 'F1, 'S', 'Workchat', 'Sarah Work' and 'Sandy'

Courts Rapist given 18 years in jail for horrific acts, but his actions since show his complete lack of remorse Hannah Mellor was raped by Ben Fitton News Paul Britton Reporter 05:58, 03 Jul 2026 Updated 07:31, 03 Jul 2026 View 4 Images The moment of A thug who subjected his ex-partner to years of abuse - including raping her - has been refused leave to appeal against his conviction and sentence Hannah Mellor - who bravely waived her right to anonymity in the aftermath of the court case - endured a 'sustained campaign of sexual, physical, emotional and psychological abuse' at the hands of Ben Fitton, police said after he was jailed. The 34 year-old was locked up for 18 y He was found guilty after a five-week trial held at Manchester Crown Court in September 2024 of two counts of rape; non-fatal strangulation; coercive and controlling behaviour; and causing grievous bodily harm The court heard Fitton had been violent towards Hannah, isolated her from friends and family, humiliated her and made various threats towards her and her family over a number of years During one row, Fitton forcefully shoved her to the ground, leaving her with a wound so deep her kneecap was visible and she had to be hospitalised. He went on to rape her on two occasions

Tameside Man, 25, who began 'seeing double' and told he has a year to live says 'I won't let this beat me' "I won’t be going down so soon without a fight" View 3 Images Jack Rawding-Revell was handed the news he had an inoperable glioblastoma (Image: News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 20:49, 02 Jul 2026 A young man who has been told he has around a year to live after he began 'seeing double' has said he won't let his shock diagnosis beat him Council worker Jack Rawding-Revell, from Hadfield, near Greater Manchester, was handed the news he had an inoperable glioblastoma after he was rushed to Salford Royal Hospital last month The 25-year-old had suffered vision loss for a number of weeks and began 'seeing double'. He went to the opticians and was prescribed glasses which temporarily helped, before he then began violently vomiting at work and knew something was wrong He called 111 and had a CT scan at Tameside hospital. He was then was rushed into Salford Royal Hospital for further tests and an operation to release pressure from his brain It was around a fortnight ago that he was called in and handed the news his condition was inoperable and would likely mean he had around a year left to live

Andy Burnham Andy Burnham vows to spend time in Manchester's No 10 North near Piccadilly as PM But he wouldn't say if he'd sanction a bank holiday if England wins the World Cup Comments News John Scheerhout Crime reporter 20:27, 02 Jul 2026 Updated 06 Andy Burnham has confirmed his new 'No 10 North' in Manchester would be situated near Piccadilly in the city centre - and that he would be based there some of the time if becomes prime minister The newly elected Makerfield MP revealed his plan during his first interview since launching his bid to succeed Sir Kier Starmer as the leader of the Labour party He repeated plans he has already made public for instance changing how Westminster and prime minister's questions - where barely any questions are answered - works and increasing business rates on warehouses and slashing them for pubs However, he also revealed he had not been told before it was announced that the government was trimming budgets elsewhere to provide an extra £15bn for defence spending And the former mayor of Greater Manchester refused to say whether he would sanction a bank holiday as one of his first acts if he were PM should England win the World Cup

New Islington 'Huge emergency response' called to New Islington Marina Water incident and enhanced rescue units also attended the scene News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 20:22, 02 Jul 2026 View Image Witnesses reported seeing a nu A 'huge emergency response' was called to New Islington Marina area of Manchester on Thursday (July 2) following reports of a fire Witnesses reported seeing a number of fire engines along with police at the scene close to Vesta Street and the Ashton Canal Tow Path It followed reports of a fire on a boat in the area. Water incident and enhanced rescue units also attended the scene following the blaze at around 2pm A spokesperson for Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service said: “At around 2:05pm this afternoon (Thursday 2 July), two fire engines, a water incident unit and two enhanced rescue units from Manchester Central , Ashton-under-Lyne and Eccles were called to a boat fire at Vesta “Firefighters arrived quickly alongside colleagues from Greater Manchester Police and North West Ambulance Service, and remained at the scene for around 50 minutes to extinguish the fire and make the area safe.”

Henri Murison: It's time to roll out the 'Manchester miracle' across Britain Henri Murison, Chief Executive of Northern Powerhouse Partnership, argues that Burnham’s latest devolution plans amount to a political revolution News opinion Henri Murison 1 Henri Murison is Chief Executive of the Northern Powerhouse Partnership and a policy leader whose work over the past decade has been closely tied to the evolution of devolution and regional growth policy in Greater Manchester. He has worked at the intersection of business, local That perspective is shaped by direct involvement in the development of the Northern Powerhouse agenda, and by close engagement with the leaders and institutions that helped turn Greater Manchester into the UK’s most advanced city-region model of devolved governance and sustained Here he writes for the Manchester Evening News on Andy Burnham's plans This week, Andy Burnham set out his stall here in Manchester at the People’s History Museum. This newspaper, the Manchester Evening News, described it on its front page as nothing short of Burnham’s Revolution - and I am inclined to agree. For the last decade I have been trying t Because what Andy set out on Monday was more than just another speech about devolution. It was an attempt to take the lessons Greater Manchester has learned over the past two decades and apply them across the country

Manchester Airport Teenage terrorist arrested at Manchester Airport Muhammad Billal researched possible shopping centres, train stations and football stadiums as he prepared to carry out a terror attack in the UK News James Holt Senior Live and Breaki A teenager who wanted to plan a terrorist attack was detained at Manchester Airport when trying to board a flight to Somalia 'to join ISIS' Muhammad Billal, from Nelson in Lancashire, had travelled to the airport on November 20 2024 to catch a plane as part of his journey to the East African country. He had spent days chatting to people who provided him with guidance on how to enter Somalia and had planned to join th The 19-year-old first started preparing to carry out a terrorist attack in the UK in February 2024. Counter Terrorism Policing North West said he gathered copies of terrorist publications and researched how to purchase knives and firearms over a number of months He also researched shooting ranges in the UK in order to practise his use of guns and scope out potential target locations. Police said it is not believed that Billal ever identified a final target, but that he had researched high footfall areas including shopping centres, train He did attempt to purchase a firearm but never gained posession of one, police said. In October 2024, he was then encouraged by a contact he had met online to cease plans for a UK based attack and instead travel to Somalia to join the violent militant group ISIS

Politics How the new voting system works for the mayoral election Everything you need to know. News Charlotte Hall Local Democracy Reporter 18:43, 02 Jul 2026 View 2 Images (Image: PA) In just under three weeks, voters across Greater Manchester will be asked elect a new mayor After Andy Burnham was elected as MP for Makerfield, he had to give up his crown as the region’s leading political figure. Mr Burnham said it was ‘such a wrench’ to leave the job ‘loves’ The decision has left a huge vacancy to fill at the Greater Manchester Combined Authority, a local government body which sits over all ten of the region’s councils. To fill the spot, the GMCA are holding a mayoral by-election on Thursday, July 30 While the vote may feel like deja vu, after residents were called to a scheduled mayoral vote just two years ago in 2024, one thing will be very different about the process this year: the voting system As of June 18, 2026, the Labour government has restored the ‘supplementary vote’ system for all mayoral votes across the country. This used to be norm in Greater Manchester in the 2017 and 2021 elections

Pictures from the scene showed a police car and fire engine blocking the main route near Barford Drive The collision was later confirmed to have been non-serious, with no injuries reported. Emergency crews were in attendance for around an hour A spokesperson for Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) said: “Just after 3.30pm this afternoon (Thursday 2nd July), one fire engine from Leigh fire station was called to reports of a road traffic collision involving two vehicles on Barford Drive, Lowton

Courts The mum who has had to bear two murder trials for her son Kelly Brown told of the horrors of losing her son View 7 Images Kelly Brown (Image: M.E.N.) News Andrew Bardsley Court reporter 18:02, 02 Jul 2026 It is a task no parent would ever want to undertake. Kelly Brown has now done it twice Her son, Rhamero West, was brutally murdered. For reasons that will likely forever remain a mystery, the 16-year-old, who that day had been due to start a college course, was stabbed to death in the street As commuters made their way home on the Princess Parkway, a violent, terrifying car chase broke out. Rhamero was in a BMW with some pals when he began to be pursued, by car and on foot, fearing for his life. He was eventually caught as he ran, taking his last breaths as he desper But it was hopeless. He collapsed in front of horrified members of the public, his fate sealed. Today, (July 2), just short of five years since the day of Rhamero’s murder, his mum again took the short walk from the public gallery in one of Manchester Crown Court's court rooms, t It was the second time she had made that short walk. Three young men were found guilty of murdering Rhamero following a trial in 2022. One of them had their conviction overturned, and then joined a new suspect at a second trial, which lasted around two weeks and ended earlier thi

Piccadilly Gardens £20m revamp of Piccadilly Gardens offices to start this month Big changes are on the way. Comments News Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 17:55, 02 Jul 2026 View 2 Images How the offices could soon look (Image: Citypress) Work is starting on a £20m overhaul of the office block at Piccadilly Gardens , which includes shutting off the cut-through to Portland Street Councillors in Manchester granted planning permission for the works in May. The walkway at the One Piccadilly Gardens building is used by thousands of people every day, but according to planning reports, it has become a magnet for trouble A report stated that anti-social behaviour issues have ‘primarily affected the ground floor entrance and walkway, causing the closure of the walkway for security reasons and the closure of the building entrance on numerous occasions, both during protests and as subsequent repairs The report added: ‘The sheltered environment of the walkthrough provides opportunity for other forms of anti-social behaviour which are a continuing nuisance to occupiers; The existing building design does not support or allow adequate control of this area.’ Overlooking Piccadilly Gardens, the office building sits at one of Manchester city centre ’s busiest locations, with close access to Piccadilly railway station, Metrolink trams, and other transport links

Lisa Nandy MP Wigan MP announces she is leaving X and taking Department for Culture with her as platform 'isn't healthy' Lisa Nandy has announced she is taking her Department off the platform as it 'favours abuse and misinformation over meaningful deb Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, and Labour MP for Wigan , Lisa Nandy has announced that she will be leaving X and taking her Department with her, saying that the platform isn't 'healthy for our democracy' The Culture Secretary made the announcement this afternoon (July 2), stating that the platform 'favours abuse and misinformation over meaningful debate' and that she doesn't 'want to support it' It comes after the Labour Party announced its plans to ban children from social media platforms, including X, on June 15, 2026 Posting onto X, Ms Nandy said: "I've decided to leave this platform and my Department will too "A platform originally designed for free speech and expression now favours abuse and misinformation over meaningful debate

And then she was groomed.” Scarlett, now 21, spoke to the Manchester Evening New s alongside her father in 2024, waiving her right to anonymity to tell her story Since then, both have spoken publicly about what happened Her case featured in the Baird Inquiry, which examined the experiences of women and girls arrested and taken into police custody in Greater Manchester. The Mancunian Way The Mancunian Way: The panic that proves Burnham’s point PLUS: When the circus leaves town News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 16:20, 02 Jul 2026 View 5 Images People on Westminster Bridge in London (Image: Ben Whitley/PA Wire) ‘Welcome to Andy’s coronation in the Rovers Return’. ‘Andy Burnham’s plan to run Britain part-time from Manchester’. ‘Northerners don’t care about your ‘No10 North’ gimmick Andy. They want a PM who can actually make their lives BETTER.’ That’s just a snapshot of the headlines in the national press this week in response to PM-in-waiting Andy Burnham ’s plans for a northern extension of No 10. Given the fuss you’d think Burnham had proposed to move Downing Street to the moon - not 200 miles up the M6 to Manchester But, writes features editor Rob Williams, the slightly hysterical reaction only proves Burnham’s point . And more importantly it demonstrates exactly why he is right to pursue it “The backlash tells us something really important,” writes Rob. “It’s a demonstration of the deep-rooted orthodoxies that Mr Burnham will have to overcome if he’s serious about relocating political power “The knee-jerk assumption that government can only properly happen within a few streets of Parliament reveals just how deeply centralisation is embedded in Britain's political culture. For all the talk of devolution, Britain remains one of the most centralised democracies in the

Victims have been told that Ahmed, who holds dual Pakistani-British citizenship, cannot be deported despite being stripped of his British citizenship This is due to a 1971 law that forbids the removal of a small group of Commonwealth citizens who arrived in the UK more than 50 years ago He is understood to have been freed on licence and told he must initially live at a bail hostel which is staffed 24 hours and wear an electronic GPS tag, so will not be allowed to his last known address on Windsor Avenue in Oldham and is subject to an 'exclusion zone', meaning he

Chadderton Fears HMO in old chippy next to nursery is turning Chadderton into 'slums’ The person behind the plans has sought to reassure people worried about the situation Comments News Edward Barnes Local Democracy Reporter 12:56, 02 Jul 2026 View Im The person behind plans to turn an Oldham chippy into a house of multiple occupation (HMO) has responded over fears HMOs were ‘turning our area into the slums’. A nearby nursery said they were ‘worried’ and parents are already concerned The former Welcome Chippy on Long Lane in Chadderton used to serve fish and chips alongside Chinese takeaways. It has been vacant for two years, according to a planning document, but now could be turned into a seven bed HMO HMOs are a house or flat is shared between three or more people who are not from the same family and can offer a more affordable form of housing. However they can be unpopular and prompt a wide range of concerns TriVantage, the agents for the application, initially put forward a eight bedroom HMO but this has to be reduced to address a number of points raised by Oldham Council. Manchester Airport Teenage terrorist arrested at Manchester Airport Muhammad Billal researched possible shopping centres, train stations and football stadiums as he prepared to carry out a terror attack in the UK News James Holt Senior Live and Breaki A teenager who wanted to plan a terrorist attack was detained at Manchester Airport when trying to board a flight to Somalia 'to join ISIS' Muhammad Billal, from Nelson in Lancashire, had travelled to the airport on November 20 2024 to catch a plane as part of his journey to the East African country. He had spent days chatting to people who provided him with guidance on how to enter Somalia and had planned to join th The 19-year-old first started preparing to carry out a terrorist attack in the UK in February 2024. Counter Terrorism Policing North West said he gathered copies of terrorist publications and researched how to purchase knives and firearms over a number of months He also researched shooting ranges in the UK in order to practise his use of guns and scope out potential target locations. Police said it is not believed that Billal ever identified a final target, but that he had researched high footfall areas including shopping centres, train He did attempt to purchase a firearm but never gained posession of one, police said. In October 2024, he was then encouraged by a contact he had met online to cease plans for a UK based attack and instead travel to Somalia to join the violent militant group ISIS

Politics How the new voting system works for the mayoral election Everything you need to know. News Charlotte Hall Local Democracy Reporter 18:43, 02 Jul 2026 View 2 Images (Image: PA) In just under three weeks, voters across Greater Manchester will be asked elect a new mayor After Andy Burnham was elected as MP for Makerfield, he had to give up his crown as the region’s leading political figure. Mr Burnham said it was ‘such a wrench’ to leave the job ‘loves’ The decision has left a huge vacancy to fill at the Greater Manchester Combined Authority, a local government body which sits over all ten of the region’s councils. To fill the spot, the GMCA are holding a mayoral by-election on Thursday, July 30 While the vote may feel like deja vu, after residents were called to a scheduled mayoral vote just two years ago in 2024, one thing will be very different about the process this year: the voting system As of June 18, 2026, the Labour government has restored the ‘supplementary vote’ system for all mayoral votes across the country. This used to be norm in Greater Manchester in the 2017 and 2021 elections

Courts The mum who has had to bear two murder trials for her son Kelly Brown told of the horrors of losing her son View 7 Images Kelly Brown (Image: M.E.N.) News Andrew Bardsley Court reporter 18:02, 02 Jul 2026 It is a task no parent would ever want to undertake. Kelly Brown has now done it twice Her son, Rhamero West, was brutally murdered. For reasons that will likely forever remain a mystery, the 16-year-old, who that day had been due to start a college course, was stabbed to death in the street As commuters made their way home on the Princess Parkway, a violent, terrifying car chase broke out. Rhamero was in a BMW with some pals when he began to be pursued, by car and on foot, fearing for his life. He was eventually caught as he ran, taking his last breaths as he desper But it was hopeless. He collapsed in front of horrified members of the public, his fate sealed. Today, (July 2), just short of five years since the day of Rhamero’s murder, his mum again took the short walk from the public gallery in one of Manchester Crown Court's court rooms, t It was the second time she had made that short walk. Three young men were found guilty of murdering Rhamero following a trial in 2022. One of them had their conviction overturned, and then joined a new suspect at a second trial, which lasted around two weeks and ended earlier thi

Piccadilly Gardens £20m revamp of Piccadilly Gardens offices to start this month Big changes are on the way. Comments News Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 17:55, 02 Jul 2026 View 2 Images How the offices could soon look (Image: Citypress) Work is starting on a £20m overhaul of the office block at Piccadilly Gardens , which includes shutting off the cut-through to Portland Street Councillors in Manchester granted planning permission for the works in May. The walkway at the One Piccadilly Gardens building is used by thousands of people every day, but according to planning reports, it has become a magnet for trouble A report stated that anti-social behaviour issues have ‘primarily affected the ground floor entrance and walkway, causing the closure of the walkway for security reasons and the closure of the building entrance on numerous occasions, both during protests and as subsequent repairs The report added: ‘The sheltered environment of the walkthrough provides opportunity for other forms of anti-social behaviour which are a continuing nuisance to occupiers; The existing building design does not support or allow adequate control of this area.’ Overlooking Piccadilly Gardens, the office building sits at one of Manchester city centre ’s busiest locations, with close access to Piccadilly railway station, Metrolink trams, and other transport links

Lisa Nandy MP Wigan MP announces she is leaving X and taking Department for Culture with her as platform 'isn't healthy' Lisa Nandy has announced she is taking her Department off the platform as it 'favours abuse and misinformation over meaningful deb Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, and Labour MP for Wigan , Lisa Nandy has announced that she will be leaving X and taking her Department with her, saying that the platform isn't 'healthy for our democracy' The Culture Secretary made the announcement this afternoon (July 2), stating that the platform 'favours abuse and misinformation over meaningful debate' and that she doesn't 'want to support it' It comes after the Labour Party announced its plans to ban children from social media platforms, including X, on June 15, 2026 Posting onto X, Ms Nandy said: "I've decided to leave this platform and my Department will too "A platform originally designed for free speech and expression now favours abuse and misinformation over meaningful debate

Oldham Council Leaderless Oldham Council met for 30 minutes, still didn't break its bitter deadlock, then watched England Oldham council remains in a deadlock amid a long stalemate Comments News Edward Barnes Local Democracy Reporter 11:47, 02 Jul 202 Oldham councillors met for half an hour, agreed one thing, and then decided to go home again. Some elected members were seen watching England play the Democratic Republic of the Congo after another meeting failed to break a bitter deadlock on who should run the authority With no one elected as Oldham Council’s leader on July 1, a long stalemate drags on. The recent local elections on May 5 left no party with a majority or even a third of seats in the council chamber Before the July 1 meeting, bids to lead had been put forward by Labour , Reform, and an alliance of different parties - all of which had been rejected. There had previously been some progress in selecting a ceremonial mayor as well as appointments to some key committees And further steps forward were made yesterday (July 1) with some appointments confirmed for council representatives at various external organisations. The only other agreement found in a divided council chamber was to again push back deciding who will be taking charge of the auth Despite a number of options on the table, leading councillors are now warning the local authority is at risk of expensive commissioners being brought in. These are public officials who take over the functions of the council and are appointed by the UK government

Today, (July 2), just short of five years since the day of Rhamero’s murder, his mum again took the short walk from the public gallery in one of Manchester Crown Court's court rooms, t It was the second time she had made that short walk. Three young men were found guilty of murdering Rhamero following a trial in 2022. A man from Lancashire who attempted to travel to Somalia to join ISIS has been jailed Muhammad Billal aged 19 of Nelson, was sentenced to 13.5 years in prison, followed by an extended license for five years, at the Central Criminal Court in London today Billal began preparing to carry out a terrorist attack in the UK. Over a number of months he gathered copies of terrorist publications and conducted detailed research on how he could purchase firearms and knives During this time, he also researched UK shooting ranges where he could practice his use of firearms and several potential target locations. It is not believed that he identified a final target but the locations researched included high footfall areas such as shopping centres, tra In October 2024, he was encouraged by a contact he had met online to cease plans for a UK based attack and instead travel to Somalia to join ISIS. At this point, Billal had made attempts to purchase a firearm but never gained possession of one Over the coming days he conducted further research on how he could enter Somalia and received advice from the individual before he was put in contact with two other people who provided guidance on travel to the country

Manchester Council’s executive is expected approve an ambitious vision for the Water Street neighbourhood at a meeting next week following overwhelming public support for proposals set out in a public consultation earlier this year The Regeneration Framework (SRF) will create a new city centre district developed around a new urban park It sets out plans for a new landscape-led neighbourhood that combines new homes, including affordable tenures, improved public spaces, enhanced walking and cycling connections, and significant new green infrastructure centred around the River Medlock Plans include a significant new city centre park accessible to residents, workers and visitors, New homes, including at least 20% affordable housing and Enhanced walking, wheeling and cycling connections linking Water Street with surrounding neighbourhoods and the city centre There will also be opportunities to create new connections to Liverpool Road, St John’s and a potential future extension of the National Trust’s Castlefield Viaduct project, helping to strengthen links across the city centre Cllr Gavin White, Manchester City Council’s executive member for housing and regeneration, said

Specialist lifting equipment will be used in the UK for the first time when upgrades to Metrolink continue this month, with tracks being replaced at Trafford Bar and Deansgate-Castlefield. Transport for Greater Manchester (TfGM) will also replace several lifts at tram stations at From Monday 13 July to Sunday 2 August, TfGM will replace around 1.6km of track – parts of which are more than 30 years old – at Trafford Bar and Deansgate-Castlefield, as well as working on a separate section on the Eccles line at the same time The track between Trafford Bar and St Peter’s Square is one of the busiest sections of light-rail in the world, with one tram passing through every 30 seconds for 20 hours a day, seven days a week While the work is being done, no trams will run on the Eccles, Trafford Centre and Altrincham lines. Services on the East Didsbury and Manchester Airport lines will terminate at Firswood, while services on the Rochdale line will terminate at Exchange Square Replacement buses will run between affected stops, with normal services due to resume from Monday 3 August. There will be a special event bus service running for the cricket at Old Trafford on 26 and 31 July (The Hundred, Manchester Super Giants vs Birmingham and then Trent Rocke Work will also take place at Eccles and the city centre around Piccadilly Gardens during this closure (13 July-29 July). This will help to reduce the need for further disruptions to the network

The special charter train will set off on Saturday 22nd August Share on WhatsApp Share via Email Rail travellers along the Mid Cheshire Line are being invited to enjoy a unique day out in August aboard The Retro Cestrian Bard, a special charter train bound for Stratford-upon-Avon Promoted by the Mid Cheshire Rail Users' Association (MCRUA) and operated by Retro Railtours, the excursion on Saturday 22nd August will pick up passengers at Altrincham, Knutsford, Northwich and Chester before heading south to the famous birthplace of William Shakespeare The return leg will take a different, more scenic route, threading through the Malvern Hills and along the Welsh Marches, giving passengers a chance to take in some of Britain's finest railway landscapes The train itself promises to be as much a part of the day out as the destination. Comfortable Mk3 air-conditioned coaches will be hauled by Class 37 diesel locomotives, with every passenger guaranteed a reserved seat Once in Stratford-upon-Avon, passengers will have around three hours to explore the town's historic streets, riverside walks, theatres and the many attractions linked to Shakespeare's life and work

The 73-year-old has been stripped of his British citizenship, leaving him solely as a Pakistani national But he cannot be deported due to a law dating back to 1971, which forbids the removal of a small group of Commonwealth citizens who arrived in the UK more than 50 years ago. Ahmed, known to his victims as 'Daddy', was released from prison yesterday (July 2) He served 14 years in prison following his conviction in 2012 for multiple rape and sexual offences against young girls. The Conservative Party says it will try to amend the Labour government's Immigration and Asylum Bill 'to close the loophole' Tory leader Kemi Badenoch said: "It's absolutely disgusting that this man has been let out. He should be deported, and we're going to do everything within our power to make sure that he is, but we're going to need the Government to get behind us. Whether it's Keir Starmer or Andy Ahmed is understood to have been freed on licence and told he must initially live at a bail hostel which is staffed 24 hours and wear an electronic GPS tag. The platform, formerly known as Twitter , was rebranded to X three years ago following Mr Musk's takeover But in the years since, the platform has come under increasing levels of scrutiny. Critics say it allows misinformation, racism and misogyny to flourish, with its algorithm often serving up hateful content to users Ms Nandy confirmed she was quitting X in a post on the platform yesterday (July 2). The Wigan MP, who is secretary of state for culture, media and sport, also said her government department would leave the site She wrote: "I've decided to leave this platform and my Department will too. A platform originally designed for free speech and expression now favours abuse and misinformation over meaningful debate. It isn't healthy for our democracy or our communities and I don’t want to support Ms Nandy said she would remain active on Instagram, Facebook and LinkedIn. The announcement means the Department for Culture, Media and Sport (DCMS) is the second government department to leave X, following the Attorney General’s office

title: Public back transformational Water Street regeneration plans with major new city centre park at its heart
summary: The Council’s executive is expected approve an ambitious vision for the Water Street neighbourhood at a meeting next week (Weds 8 July) following overwhelming public support for proposals set out in a public consultation earlier this year
lead: The Council’s executive is expected approve an ambitious vision for the Water Street neighbourhood at a meeting next week (Weds 8 July) following overwhelming public support for proposals set out in a public consultation earlier this year
practical_angle: Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня.
raw_evidence: The Council’s executive is expected approve an ambitious vision for the Water Street neighbourhood at a meeting next week (Weds 8 July) following overwhelming public support for proposals set out in a public consultation earlier this year More than 90% of respondents supported the key principles of the draft Water Street Strategic Regeneration Framework (SRF) update that will create a new city centre district developed around a new urban park The SRF is a high-level blueprint for future investment that will guide development through the planning process. The SRF– sets out plans for a new landscape-led neighbourhood that combines new homes, including affordable tenures, improved public spaces, enhanced walking and cycl The consultation responses from residents, businesses and stakeholders, expressed particularly strong support for the environmental and public realm improvements proposed for the site The updated framework responds to the significant transformation that has already taken place around Water Street in recent years, including nearby developments such as St John’s, Trinity Islands, Potato Wharf, Enterprise City, Aviva Studios and Campfield Building on this success, Water Street now presents a strategic opportunity to create a distinctive new neighbourhood that complements surrounding communities and supports Manchester's continued growth, while providing opportunities for recreation and wellbeing, and reconnect peo

title: Investing in jobs - how Council's £3m plan will help Mancunians to share in success
summary: Manchester City Council is set to invest almost £3 million in new initiatives to help more of our residents share in the success of the city’s economic growth
lead: Manchester City Council is set to invest almost £3 million in new initiatives to help more of our residents share in the success of the city’s economic growth
practical_angle: Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня.
raw_evidence: Manchester City Council is set to invest almost £3 million in new initiatives to help more of our residents share in the success of the city’s economic growth The schemes are designed to get hundreds more young people into apprenticeships and jobs and help many adults gain the skills and qualifications they need to progress in better paid and more secure jobs – especially in key growth industries within the city The three-year initiatives, to run until 2028/29, will be paid for from the Council’s Prevention Fund – money set aside in the budget for invest-to-save initiatives which will help reduce future demands on council support services Deputy Council Leader Cllr Garry Bridges said: “Manchester’s economy is a real success story. Over the past decade it’s grown twice as fast as the UK’s economy as a whole. We are determined to ensure that this growth benefits everyone in the city and that’s what this £3m skills i “There’s too much talent wasted in our city - people being held back from doing the jobs they could be doing, because they don’t have the right skills or qualifications. Some young people face barriers which stop them even getting on the first rung of the ladder, and can lead to “We can’t and won’t accept this. This investment will strengthen the support to people living and working in our city, to help young people get good jobs and help people get the skills they need to progress in their careers and reap the financial rewards of moving up the career l

A convicted murderer has admitted kidnapping a 15-year-old girl at knifepoint and sexually assaulting her Paul Sharpe subjected his victim to a protracted ordeal which included tying up her wrists and feet, abusing her and performing a sexual act on himself, the Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) said The incident, which happened in May, only ended when police arrived and arrested Sharpe some eight hours later The 56-year-old, from Stockport, Greater Manchester, pleaded guilty at Manchester's Minshull Street Crown Court to six offences including kidnapping, sexual assault and threats with a blade, and is due to be sentenced at the same court on 4 September Sharpe first put the girl in a headlock and forced her into his car in Altrincham on 18 May at about 19:30 BST He claimed a relative owed him money and drove to an airport car park before tying up her wrists and ankles

A man has been found guilty of raping two 14-year-old girls after "plying" them with alcohol in his flat Sultani Bakatash, 29, from Bolton, met up with his two victims after first messaging on social media and taking them to his home on 6 December last year He encouraged the girls to drink alcohol until they were drifting in and out of consciousness, before raping and sexually assaulting both girls, the Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) said Bakatash, who police described as an Afghan national, denied the charges but was convicted following a trial at Bolton Crown Court After the attack the girls left the flat and sought help, and police were contacted Bakatash had left his home but he was found the following morning at his brother's house, the CPS said

A woman who stabbed her husband, who had dementia, with a kitchen knife and then lied that he had accidentally fallen on it has been jailed Daryl Berman told police that David Berman, 84, had stumbled while he took a lunch tray into the kitchen of their home in Prestwich, Bury, on 13 March last year Berman, 72, claimed her husband of 27 years had said, "It's OK, I slipped" before she heard a groan and she ran in to discover him face down and covered in blood Her account was initially accepted but her actions "unravelled" when suspicions were raised about the injury and a post-mortem examination later concluded it was non-accidental. She was sentenced to life with a minimum of 12 years Manchester Minshull Street Crown Court heard retired joiner Mr Berman also had a stab wound to his right middle finger, which prosecutors said was a common self-defence injury Paramedics attended the couple's home on Butterstile Lane after the defendant dialled 999 but they were unable to save her husband

We need the support of everyone, especially the children." There is a 30mph speed limit on the busy main road The 84-year-old was one of the first people to call for action against racing cars on Grimshaw Lane, but she feels the proposed plans will aggravate the problem rather than solve it The initial designs have shown plans for three pedestrian crossing islands; bus stop improvements and double yellow lines at blind spots The double yellow lines and an altered bus stop near where Grimshaw Lane meets Sandy Lane would mean Ann would lose her parking space. She appeared on TV just days after her former partner, Michael Cosgrove, had been sentenced to 20 years in prison following his conviction for attempted murder an The 46-year-old of no fixed address, but previously of Wythenshawe , was told he would serve two-thirds of his term behind bars. So when Kerry received a voicemail from a victim liaison officer informing her that Cosgrove may now serve only six years in prison, she felt her world "I listened to it and I felt sick," Kerry told the Manchester Evening News . "I couldn't work out what was going on "I feel bad because it was nothing to do with her, but it was just so matter of fact - like 'it's happening, there's nothing you can do, get used to it'. I feel like when he does get released, because it's only six years, he will be here in no time."

Prestwich Moment wife's lies started minutes after murdering her husband Daryl Berman has been handed a life sentence for murdering her husband David Berman News Stephen Topping Senior reporter and Amy Walker Court reporter 13:27, 03 Jul 2026 Updated Stood outside the Prestwich home where her husband had been fatally stabbed, Daryl Berman asked a police officer: "You don't think I've murdered him, do you?" M60 M60 to close for two weekends as 'essential' works take place Major works are due to take place on the M60 motorway Comments News Stephen Topping Senior reporter 12:20, 03 Jul 2026 View Image Part of the M60 in Tameside will close (Image: Manchest Part of the M60 will shut in Tameside on two consecutive weekends as 'essential improvements' are made to the road. National Highways is partway through a series of drainage and carriageway repairs between junctions 23 at Ashton-under-Lyne and 24 at Denton , which began in Januar As the works continue, National Highways says the anticlockwise carriageway will fully close from 9pm on Friday, July 10, until 6am on Saturday, July 11. The motorway will then reopen, but with one lane closed throughout Saturday until 9pm The M60 will then fully close again anticlockwise between junctions 23 and 24 from 9pm on Saturday, July 11, until 6am on Monday, July 13. On the following weekend, works will focus on the clockwise carriageway, with further closures in place The clockwise side will be fully closed between junctions 23 and 24 from 9am on Friday, July 17, until 6am on Saturday, July 18. As in the previous weekend, the motorway will reopen on the Saturday morning, with one lane remaining shut until 9pm From 9pm, the clockwise side will close fully again until 6am on Monday, July 20. Additionally, the anticlockwise carriageway will also be closed from midnight until 6am on Monday morning, July 6

Bolton Horwich North by-election result in full as hyperlocal party triumphs Horwich & Blackrod First Independent candidate John Scoble registered more votes that other six contenders put together Comments News Chris Gee Local democracy reporter 10:55 A by-election for a single seat on Bolton council has been won by hyper-local party Horwich & Blackrod First Independents (HBFI). Buses The £2, 90 minute bus journey along Greater Manchester's most stunning route From the top deck, you can take in 90 minutes of the region's most stunning scenery Comments News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 06:04, 04 Jul 2026 Updated 07:12, 04 Jul 2026 Or you could buy a ticket for a 90 minute trip on what's arguably Greater Manchester's most scenic bus route That's exactly what I did this week when I caught the Bee Network's 350 bus from Oldham to Ashton . And the half-hourly service really does go the long way round From Oldham bus station it heads east into the picturesque villages of Saddleworth before horseshoeing southwest into Mossley and downhill into Ashton. If you're in a rush, maybe get a cab Luckily I'm not and after paying the driver I head to the top deck and take a seat right at the front, so I've got a widescreen view of the ever-changing scenery. We're on our way within moments, minus a quick stop near the market to let off a fella who's caught the wrong bus As we trundle out of town from Oldham Mumps we pass the handsome old Co-op building on Huddersfield Road, now home to a Carpetworld promising 'Great laminate deals in store now' and the excellently-named Knuckles Muay Thai boxing gym

True crime series 'Life means life': The infamous Greater Manchester killers told they will die in prison 'Just punishment in your case requires you to be kept in prison for the rest of your life' View 12 Images Whole life orders have been around sinc News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 06:04, 04 Jul 2026 "I do not think that either of these prisoners should ever be released from custody," Margaret Thatcher wrote to her then Home Secretary Leon Brittan in 1985. "Their crime was the most hideous and cruel in modern times." Thatcher was referring to Moors Murderers Ian Brady and Myra Hindley . And five years later her wish came true In 1990 Hindley became the first woman in England and Wales to be given a whole-life term, 24 years after she and Brady were first sentenced to life in prison for the sexual abuse, torture and murder of three children. She joined a relatively small number of criminals serving the Whole life orders have been around since 1983. The Sentencing Council says they are for only the most serious cases of murder There were 74 whole-life prisoners in England and Wales as of September 2025, according to Ministry of Justice statistics. Several other inmates have died while serving a whole-life term

Middleton The 'racing boys' of Grimshaw Lane that have divided a community Boy racers have been a problem on Grimsahw Lane for years, but the proposal to solve the issue has proved controversial Comments News George Lythgoe Local Democracy Reporter 06 They shake the houses as they rattle past at high speeds. Some call them the ‘racing boys’ of Grimshaw Lane, and one Middletonian pensioner has had enough Ann Lewis has lived on the long stretch of road for years and says cars speeding down the road at around 60mph has been a bugbear for a long time. There is a 30mph speed limit on the busy main road The 84-year-old was one of the first people to call for action against racing cars on Grimshaw Lane, but she feels the proposed plans will aggravate the problem rather than solve it The initial designs have shown plans for three pedestrian crossing islands; bus stop improvements and double yellow lines at blind spots The double yellow lines and an altered bus stop near where Grimshaw Lane meets Sandy Lane would mean Ann would lose her parking space. She has mobility issues following a recent stroke, so she fears she won’t be able to cross the road to get to her newly proposed disabled parking

Crime Woman's bravery put her partner behind bars for 20 years, until a voicemail turned her world upside down A voicemail turned Kerry Allan's world upside down View 6 Images Cosgrove is one of thousands of prisoners who could become eligible for ear Comments News Stephen Topping Senior reporter 06:04, 04 Jul 2026 Updated 07:13, 04 Jul 2026 A woman strangled to the point of unconsciousness by her then-partner says she has been left 'heartbroken' after discovering he could be released from prison early Kerry Allan bravely spoke out to encourage other women who had survived abuse to share their stories with the police. She appeared on TV just days after her former partner, Michael Cosgrove, had been sentenced to 20 years in prison following his conviction for attempted murder an The 46-year-old of no fixed address, but previously of Wythenshawe , was told he would serve two-thirds of his term behind bars. So when Kerry received a voicemail from a victim liaison officer informing her that Cosgrove may now serve only six years in prison, she felt her world "I listened to it and I felt sick," Kerry told the Manchester Evening News . "I couldn't work out what was going on "I feel bad because it was nothing to do with her, but it was just so matter of fact - like 'it's happening, there's nothing you can do, get used to it'. I feel like when he does get released, because it's only six years, he will be here in no time."

World Cup England vs Mexico could be moved to earlier kick-off, reports say Fifa has yet to confirm or deny the reports Comments News Greta Simpson Senior Reporter 20:18, 03 Jul 2026 Updated 20:18, 03 Jul 2026 View Image England players at the end of The World Cup clash between England and Mexico could be moved to an earlier kick-off time, the BBC has reported The broadcaster said that Fifa is understood to be bringing forward the game in Mexico City to 12pm local time (7pm UK time). It had been scheduled to kick off at 01:00 UK time on Monday morning (6pm local time) Weather forecasts indicate that there could be thunderstorms and heavy rain in the city on Sunday evening, although Fifa has not yet officially commented to confirm or deny the reports Fifa regulations say that they have the right to "cancel, reschedule or relocate" matches "at its sole discretion" The Guardian reported that Mexico’s round of 32 match against Ecuador was delayed due to rain, while France’s group game against Iraq in Philadelphia was interrupted for two hours because of thunderstorms

Courts Alleged murderer tells jury he 'just wanted to get sister's boyfriend out of the house' Anthony Junior Murray, 25, denies murdering 23-year-old Joe Burton News Amy Walker Court reporter 19:30, 03 Jul 2026 View 2 Images Joe Burton (Image: Greate A man accused of stabbing his sister's boyfriend to death on New Year's Eve has denied intending to kill him, telling jurors that he 'just wanted to get him out of the house' Anthony Junior Murray, 25, denies murdering 23-year-old Joe Burton in Bury. It is alleged that there was a mix of 'intoxication, lateness of the hour' and tensions between their two families that preceded the incident Mr Murray allegedly lied to police and denied responsibility in the aftermath, jurors at Minshull Street Crown Court have heard. But in a 'remarkable stroke of luck', the stabbing was captured on a camera designed to monitor Mr Murray's two dogs, the court heard Giving evidence from the witness box, Mr Murray claimed there had been previous issues with Mr Burton being 'aggressive' in the past. During one incident over Christmas 2024, he said Mr Burton had been asked to leave the family home after 'swearing in front of the children' "Before there was a fight, how many times was he asked to leave?" asked his barrister, Bernard Richmond KC

Salford Royal hospital Salford Royal Hospital staff strike in overtime payments dispute The union says that staff feel 'forced' to take on extra shifts that pay lesser rates News Ramazani Mwamba News reporter 18:59, 03 Jul 2026 View 3 Images Staff say Staff at Salford Royal Hospital have gone on strike in a dispute regarding overtime pay. Local politicians and union reps joined nurses, practitioners, nursing associates, clinical support workers, ward clerks and housekeepers on the picket line this morning (July 3) Demonstrators claim their employer, Northern Care Alliance Trust (NCA), has failed to pay the rates for overtime shifts agreed as per their NHS terms and conditions According to Unison, the trust had agreed that overtime pay would be 1.5x for regular weekdays and double time on bank holidays However, the union says the NCA has found a way to circumvent the agreement by placing the extra shifts on their in-house system, which allows the trust to pay staff at a lower rate NCA said it is disappointed the action is taking place and said they are working hard to minimise any disruption

Bev Craig Bev Craig wants a Greater Manchester that thrives and gets things done but won't say yet how much it will cost Despite struggling to start for more than three minutes, the Bev Battle Bus was eventually off as Labour look to take the battle t Labour’s candidate for Mayor of Greater Manchester Bev Craig says she will help build ‘a Greater Manchester that thrives’ and ‘get stuff done’, but is yet to outline how much her proposals for public transport will cost At a campaign event on July 3, Ms Craig announced several key transport policies she hopes to deliver if she is elected on July 30 with the launch of the ‘Bev Battle Bus’. Despite struggling to start for more than three minutes, the bus was eventually off as Labour look to take t Speaking at the Burnage Rugby Football Club just around the corner from the East Didsbury Metrolink stop, she said: “I would not make any promises I couldn’t deliver so everything I’ll set out to the Greater Manchester people are things that are fully costed, worked through, and This is in reference to this month’s vote being a special election to replace former Mayor Andy Burnham who is now the Makerfield MP. Voters will go to the polls again in 2028 to elect their next mayor for a full term Ms Craig said she would freeze bus fares and keep a £2 cap as well as extending bus passes including the Our Pass which she says could put up to £500 into family’s pockets. She also announced she would be expanding bus routes ‘to make sure every town across Greater Manchester has

Courts He picked up the phone to his mum then knew his dad's life was at risk Daryl, 72, was jailed for life for the murder of husband David Berman, 84 News Amy Walker Court reporter 18:18, 03 Jul 2026 Updated 20:33, 03 Jul 2026 Police bodycam shows w Dad and grandfather David Berman, 84, had been to soft play with his daughter Debbie Davis and her grandchildren But the day ended in tragedy. He went home, had his lunch, then lost his life Minutes later, David's son, Daryl, received a call from David's wife, also called Daryl. As soon as he heard her voice, he knew something was wrong It would later be revealed that Mrs Berman, then 71, had stabbed her husband in the chest with a 'little paring knife' she had used for her lunch. She would go on to claim that he had fallen whilst carrying her lunch tray back into the kitchen Ultimately, a jury convicted her of his murder. Today, July 3, Berman, now 72, was jailed for life to serve a minimum term of 12 years

Greater Manchester mayoral election 2026 Green Party pledges 20,000 homes for Greater Manchester - but is it realistic? Geraldine Coggins, the Green Party candidate for mayor, announced plans for a housing 'revolution' in the region during a roundtabl News Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 17:49, 03 Jul 2026 Updated 18:49, 03 Jul 2026 From the streets of Hulme, Greater Manchester’s housing problems are glaringly obvious. Standing here, the glass skyscrapers towering above the region come into view. Many of these buildings But to live in such a place requires stumping up rent upwards of £2,000 a month according to several listings on property websites. For many people in Greater Manchester, that’s not far away from a monthly salary Meanwhile, tens of thousands of people across the region are sitting on housing registers, desperately waiting for a home to call their own, including here in Hulme To address the disconnect, the Green Party are proposing a ‘revolutionary’ solution Their summary of Greater Manchester’s housing problem is something many in the region may recognise: “The economy is ostensibly thriving on paper, while people aren’t thriving. People here are being left behind.”

Courts Horror as police descend on hotel car park to find missing girl kidnapped by murderer Paul Sharpe threatened his victim at knifepoint and sexually assaulted her Comments News Greta Simpson Senior Reporter 21:15, 03 Jul 2026 Updated 06:44, 04 Ju A convicted murderer from Stockport has pleaded guilty to a string of offences after subjecting a 15-year-old girl to a terrifying ordeal in which he kidnapped her at knifepoint and held her hostage for hours in his car Paul Sharpe, from Portwood, pleaded guilty at Minshull Street Crown Court today (July 3) to six offences including kidnapping, sexual assault, sexual activity in the presence of a child and making threats with a bladed article Police discovered the 56-year-old in a hotel car park in Knutsford in the early hours of May 19 this year with the girl in his vehicle, which he had forced her into on Bradgate Road in Altrincham the previous evening, the Crown Prosecution Service said The service said it was a 'protracted' and 'terrifying' ordeal where Sharpe, said to be a convicted murderer, tied up her wrists and feet and masturbated in her presence The kidnapping took place at around 7:30pm on May 18 when Sharpe threatened her with a knife, put her in a headlock and forced her into his car

Courts Police catch 'em all as brothers who stole Pokémon cards worth £62k convicted Detectives said 'overwhelming evidence' was collected to implicate them in break-ins at specialist stores Comments News Paul Britton Reporter 17:28, 03 Jul 2026 Updat Two brothers stole Pokémon cards worth more than £62,000 in raids on specialist shops, police have revealed Keith Johnson, 33, and his elder brother Shane, 37, have both pleaded guilty to two counts of burglary with intent to steal, Cheshire Police said. The brothers targeted Celestial Collectables on Lovely Lane in Warrington, Cheshire, in April this year Police said the shop specialises in rare and high-value Pokémon trading cards along with memorabilia highly sought after by collectors In a statement, the force said the store's window was smashed and it was left ransacked, with Pokémon cards left strewn across the floor A crowbar used to smash the glass in the front door and shelving and cabinets was recovered from the scene

Shaw Shaw church is crumbling and a danger to the public - but hundreds are fighting plans to restore it Part of the church in Shaw collapsed in 2019 and later parts of it were demolished due to concerns around public safety Comments News Edward Barne Hundreds of people are fighting against plans to renovate a Grade II-listed church in Shaw that has partially collapsed. The chapel was abandoned in 1975 and is considered 'dangerous' but could have a new future under plans put forward The chapel at St Paul’s Methodist Church on Rochdale Road in Shaw was built in 1863 with a number of school and nursery buildings added over the decades. The church has had a long history but after years of being closed, the roof of the chapel partially collapsed in February 2019 Months before the collapse, in 2018, Joanne Balforth, the Methodist Conservation officer, had warned a collapse of the building ‘would be catastrophic as it is near houses, a main road, and there is a nursery operating from the site’. Works were carried out in 2019 following the The building is considered ‘extremely unsafe’ and while some ‘fragments of the decorative plasterwork frieze remain’. Scaffolding was put up ‘to protect the public’ as ‘the perilous state of the building and the risk to the public mean that there is not time to consider the futur Plans have now been put forward to carry out further works to the building. Mohammadia Masjid and Islamic Centre bought the site in 2021 in the hopes of establishing ‘a community social and religious assembly’. Courts Alleged murderer tells jury he 'just wanted to get sister's boyfriend out of the house' Anthony Junior Murray, 25, denies murdering 23-year-old Joe Burton News Amy Walker Court reporter 19:30, 03 Jul 2026 View 2 Images Joe Burton (Image: Greate A man accused of stabbing his sister's boyfriend to death on New Year's Eve has denied intending to kill him, telling jurors that he 'just wanted to get him out of the house' Anthony Junior Murray, 25, denies murdering 23-year-old Joe Burton in Bury. It is alleged that there was a mix of 'intoxication, lateness of the hour' and tensions between their two families that preceded the incident Mr Murray allegedly lied to police and denied responsibility in the aftermath, jurors at Minshull Street Crown Court have heard. Courts He picked up the phone to his step-mum then knew his dad's life was at risk Daryl, 72, was jailed for life for the murder of husband David Berman, 84 News Amy Walker Court reporter 18:18, 03 Jul 2026 Updated 13:50, 04 Jul 2026 Police bodycam sh Dad and grandfather David Berman, 84, had been to soft play with his daughter Debbie Davis and her grandchildren But the day ended in tragedy. He went home, had his lunch, then lost his life Minutes later, David's son, Daryl, received a call from David's wife, also called Daryl. As soon as he heard her voice, he knew something was wrong It would later be revealed that Mrs Berman, then 71, had stabbed her husband in the chest with a 'little paring knife' she had used for her lunch. She would go on to claim that he had fallen whilst carrying her lunch tray back into the kitchen Ultimately, a jury convicted her of his murder. Today, July 3, Berman, now 72, was jailed for life to serve a minimum term of 12 years

Geraldine Coggins, the Green Party candidate for mayor, announced plans for a housing 'revolution' in the region during a roundtabl News Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 17:49, 03 Jul 2026 Updated 18:49, 03 Jul 2026 From the streets of Hulme, Greater Manchester’s housing problems are glaringly obvious. Standing here, the glass skyscrapers towering above the region come into view. Many of these buildings But to live in such a place requires stumping up rent upwards of £2,000 a month according to several listings on property websites. For many people in Greater Manchester, that’s not far away from a monthly salary Meanwhile, tens of thousands of people across the region are sitting on housing registers, desperately waiting for a home to call their own, including here in Hulme To address the disconnect, the Green Party are proposing a ‘revolutionary’ solution Their summary of Greater Manchester’s housing problem is something many in the region may recognise: “The economy is ostensibly thriving on paper, while people aren’t thriving. People here are being left behind.”

As in the previous weekend, the motorway will reopen on the Saturday morning, with one lane remaining shut until 9pm From 9pm, the clockwise side will close fully again until 6am on Monday, July 20. A woman who stabbed her 84-year-old husband with a vegetable knife at their home in Prestwich has been handed a life sentence with a minimum of 12 years in prison for murder Daryl Berman aged 72, of Butterstile Lane, Prestwich, was found guilty following an eight-day trial at Minshull Street Crown Court which ended on Tuesday The court heard how on Thursday 13 March 2026, Greater Manchester Police received a report from the North West Ambulance Service in relation to an incident at Butterstile Lane, Prestwich When officer arrived at the scene, paramedics were working on the victim – David Berman – in the kitchen of the address.
