Drugs gangs are flooding the north of England with cheap cannabis grown overseas and sold to mimic slickly marketed, legal marijuana from the United States, a BBC investigation has learned A thriving black market for "Cali weed" - high-potency strains originally cultivated in licensed dispensaries in California - has prompted an unprecedented wave of smuggling from dealers growing it in countries including Thailand One dealer boasted he made massive profits importing cannabis and described how he sent drug couriers from Thailand to the UK, even though he knew "most will get caught" According to Home Office figures seen by the BBC, Border Force officials last year seized more than 167,000kg of herbal cannabis with an estimated street value of more than £2bn - the highest amount ever recorded and up 52% from 2024 The haul - equivalent to two fully loaded large passenger jets - included 28 tonnes of cannabis flown in suitcases by 800 couriers who were arrested by the National Crime Agency (NCA) Suppliers are running industrial-sized operations in parts of the world where laws governing the cultivation of cannabis have been relaxed - including Thailand, Canada and parts of the US - and illegally importing multiple-kilogram loads to the UK for "huge profits"

A man deliberately drove his car at a 15-year-old boy who had stolen an e-bike in an act of revenge, a trial has heard It has been alleged that Terrence King intentionally struck Devon Simmonds-Caines on Rowrah Crescent in Middleton, Greater Manchester, on 22 July 2025 Michael Hayton KC, outlining the prosecution's case at Manchester's Minshull Street Crown Court, said it "was not a tragic motor accident or poor driving" The jury was told King, 60, had admitted manslaughter and accepted his actions caused Devon's death, but he denied murder CCTV and doorbell camera footage showed Simmonds-Caines riding an e-bike, which the court heard he had stolen from another boy King's car was seen driving at speed on the road before veering left, mounting the pavement and crashing into Simmonds-Caines, who died at the scene from his injuries

title: Independent Marcus Farmer in third Greater Manchester mayor poll
summary: Independent candidate Marcus Farmer is to stand in his third Greater Manchester mayoral election The businessman came sixth out of nine candidates in 2021, receiving just under 1% of the vote with 6,448 votes Four years earlier he came sixth out of six polling 3,360 which was .59
lead: Independent candidate Marcus Farmer is to stand in his third Greater Manchester mayoral election The businessman came sixth out of nine candidates in 2021, receiving just under 1% of the vote with 6,448 votes Four years earlier he came sixth out of six polling 3,360 which was .59
practical_angle: Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня.
raw_evidence: Independent candidate Marcus Farmer is to stand in his third Greater Manchester mayoral election The businessman came sixth out of nine candidates in 2021, receiving just under 1% of the vote with 6,448 votes Four years earlier he came sixth out of six polling 3,360 which was .59% of the poll The election, which takes place on 30 July, was triggered because former mayor Andy Burnham was elected as MP for Makerfield Tell us which stories we should cover in Greater Manchester Contact form Contact form Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , external , X , external , and Instagram , external . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

North African football fans in Greater Manchester have been gearing up for Egypt and Morocco's World Cup with Argentina and France Maz and Ingy, an Egyptian couple from Cheadle, are preparing to host friends at their home on Tuesday for the quarter final against the South American side Maz, a pharmacist and dual national, said he had enjoyed watching both England and Egypt progress in the tournament A Moroccan fan awaiting Thursday's game against France said they would be supporting Mo Salah's side as they took on Lionel Messi's men when the Egypt - Argentina game kicks off in Atlanta at 17:00 BST "I feel incredibly lucky, if anything a little bit spoiled, to not only be able to support and cheer for both teams, but watch them shine," Maz said He said the family would be gathering "Egyptian Mancunians" at their house after work for a watch party, with snacks ready and Egypt shirts on

A coroner has called for action to prevent "a significant risk of future deaths" after a grandmother died when a wall collapsed onto her while she was in a lean-to carport below Clarice Berry, 77, died from chest injuries after the gable end wall of her home in Shevington, Wigan, fell on to her in July 2021, an inquest in Bolton heard Coroner Bronia Hartley called on landlord S&G Properties (No2 Ltd) and KMPM property management company to ensure such an incident does not happen again Landlord Shlomo Klein said they were really upset about Clarice Berry's death and would "do anything that we can to prevent anything like this happening again". KMPM has been approached for comment Clarice Berry had been under a lean-to carport on 18 July 2021 when the gable end wall and the upper part of the outer leaf of the end wall of her home weighing about 3,800 kg (3.8 tonnes) collapsed on to her, trapping her beneath the rubble An inquest in Bolton on 18 June heard she was freed by firefighters and taken to the Royal Albert Edward Infirmary in Wigan, where she died later the same day

An 18-year-old man who died after a car smashed into a tree was a "fantastic son" who "touched so many lives", his family have said George Gaffney, from Northwich, was a front seat passenger in a grey Vauxhall Corsa which hit a tree on Holmes Chapel Road, Allostock, at about 21:40 BST on Sunday, Cheshire Police said His family said: "During his short life he touched so many lives with his personality and charm, and he will be greatly remembered by all those who knew him." George was pronounced dead at the scene, while two other teenagers have been injured, one critically, in the incident "It is with great difficulty and sadness that we say goodbye to our fantastic son George," the family tribute said "George was a well-loved member of the Northwich community; he had recently completed his A levels at Sir John Deanes college and was excited to embark on a new career in business finance."

Tameside Tameside residents are underpaid, cut-off and dying young - but there's a plan to change the grim fate The new boroughwide strategy is due to e voted on next week Comments News George Lythgoe Local Democracy Reporter 06:04, 08 Jul 2026 View 3 People in Tameside are paid less, feel cut-off and don’t live as long. Now the local authority has a strategy to change all that Next week’s full council will officially vote on the Tameside Borough Strategy – which aims to improve the lives of residents. The strategy largely looks at playing to the strengths of the borough’s nine individual towns and increasing housebuilding, job opportunities and improvi Not enough people in Tameside earn above the national minimum wage and lack opportunities, according to the council strategy. There is a desire to increase the level of support for people to find work and gain skills and qualifications Health and happiness makes up a large proportion of the borough’s issues. A fifth of homes in the borough were built over a century ago, meaning they are less efficient and cost residents more to heat Life expectancy is also low and ‘too many people die of preventable illnesses’, due to poor physical and mental health, say council bosses. In addition to this, the strategy document says residents feel more cut off from high-quality culture and leisure activities and they feel u

House Prices House prices are soaring, see how your postcode compares with our interactive map A total of 49 areas of Greater Manchester saw average prices increase in the last year Comments News Cullen Willis , David Dubas-Fisher Data Investigations Part of Salford is currently Greater Manchester's property hotspot, according to the latest figures Homes in the M6 postcode, which includes Claremont, Irlams O' Th' Height, Charlestown, and Whit Lane, sold for an average of £238,033 each in the 12 months to the end of May That’s an increase of £48,523, 25.6 percent, compared to a year earlier, the largest increase of any postcode area in our region with 20 or more house sales Our survey of property prices using Land Registry Data includes every postcode area which is either fully in Greater Manchester, or where a part of it is in the region. The SK1 area of central Stockport saw the next highest rise. Properties each sold for an average of £231,855 th The M9 postcode, including Harpurhey and Blackley , with an annual increase of 13.pc

Courts Moment 97-year-old is hugged and kissed by women in Manchester before their disgusting ruse became clear They have now been jailed News Chris Slater Senior Reporter 06:01, 08 Jul 2026 Updated 07:19, 08 Jul 2026 Three women jailed for string of The vulnerable 97 year-old victim, who uses a walking aid, was targeted as she waited for her husband to pick her up on Dale Street in Manchester city centre on January 10 this year She was approached by prolific thieves Geta Schian, 33, and Sefora Mazarache, 27, who began to hold her hand, hug her and kiss her However it was all a ruse to distract her as they stole a jade antique bracelet off her hand. They were captured on clear CCTV footage which formed part of the compelling case against them It followed a string of thefts and attempted thefts involving the women, and a third defendant, 35 year-old Geneza Dumitru, one of which happened less than two hours beforehand Police said they 'worked together to distract, charm and steal from lone women in the community.'

Manchester City Centre Plan for new floating walkways in Manchester making ‘significant’ progress Once just an idea, steps are being taken to turn an exciting vision into reality. Comments News Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 05:57, 08 Jul 2026 Punters eating out in Manchester city centre could one day head home on a floating walkway along the River Irwell It might sound like a vision of the distant future, but bosses leading a project to build a 100-mile network of brightly-coloured walking and cycling routes across the city say the plan is making real progress The proposal, known as CyanLines, could completely change how residents and commuters trek around Manchester “We are well underway with agreeing and co-designing the first phase of CyanLines routes with our partners, including building our investment case for them,” said Tom Bloxham, chair of property developer Urban Splash and a co-founder of CyanLines The next step, Bloxham explained, is to ‘lobby for national and international funding’ to get things really moving. A vision for the scheme was first announced in September . It aims to spend £100m building a network of floating walking routes based around the city’s waterways

Oldham Man left with serious injuries after being hit by car following 'short police pursuit' in Oldham Officers followed a car that was being 'driven dangerously' before the collision News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 20:55, 07 J A man was left with serious injuries after being hit by a car following a short pursuit by police in Oldham on Tuesday evening (July 7) Officers in Oldham had been made aware of a car being driven dangerously shortly before 7pm. The car involved then passed a police car 'at speed', Greater Manchester Police said A witness described how the police vehicle then 'spun around' to follow them and engaged in a short pursuit. It's understood that within a matter of seconds, a pedestrian had then been knocked down The pedestrian, a man, was left with serious injuries along Park Road in the Glodwick area. He was rushed to hospital from the scene View 3 Images Officers followed a car that was being 'driven dangerously' before the collision (Image: ASP)

Oldham He went out to watch the Euros final - it cost him his life "Ray never stopped fighting to get better for the last two years. All we can wish for now is that he gets the justice he deserves" View 6 Images Ramon Logan had been out to watch the E News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 20:07, 07 Jul 2026 Ramon Logan had been out to watch the Euros final. It was July 14 2024 and he had watched England take on Spain at a local rugby club before heading out for drinks in Oldham Just hours later he was rushed to hospital in a critical condition having been the victim of a brutal attack in the early hours of the following morning. He was punched to the ground and violently assaulted After two years fighting for his life, the dad-of-four tragically died aged 42 on June 30. His wife Charlotte said in a social media statement that Ramon, also known to his loved ones as Ray, had 'fought so hard' and is now 'at peace' following his gruelling battle Ramon had spent time in hospital with serious and life-changing injuries following the assault. His attacker, Sebastian Jones, was later jailed . A court heard how he launched into the assault at around 4am Footage had captured the moment Jones left a nightclub, before heading towards Ramon and punching him. The force from the attack caused the dad to fall to the ground, where Jones continued to assault him as he lay defenceless

Stockport Stockport man accuses council of stabbing him in the back like Julius Caesar The local authority however argues the purchase was necessary to move a £145m development forward. Comments News Edward Barnes Local Democracy Reporter 20:00, 07 Ju A Stockport man has accused his own council of ‘stabbing a knife in the back’ after they bought his property against his will. The local authority however argues the purchase was necessary to move a £145m development forward In June 2025, Stockport Council’s cabinet made a decision to use compulsory purchase order powers to buy up 72 and 74 Wellington Road South in the town, owned by local solicitor Kieran Henry. These powers allow councils and other public organisations to buy up land or property wi This was being done to move forward the Stockport Exchange scheme , a huge employment development , that the local authority said is ‘critical to the continued growth of the town’. The development includes plans for new offices, homes, and retail as well as leisure facilities A report before councillors said the council had bought all the land in the area up except for the site on Wellington Road South, which ‘acts as a barrier between the town centre and the railway station’ and was holding the entire £145m scheme back. The council want to demolish t The report said: “Despite the council’s sustained efforts to engage and negotiate with the landowner over a prolonged period of over 10 years, voluntary agreement for the acquisition of the site has not been possible.”

Snake Pass 'Avoid area' warning in place after serious crash on Snake Pass closes road The busy route was closed off by police with an air ambulance also reportedly called to the scene News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 19:52, 07 J An 'avoid the area' warning has been put in place after a serious crash on the Snake Pass on Tuesday evening (July 7) The scenic and busy route through the Peak District from Glossop was closed in both directions by Derbyshire Police as they responded to the crash The force urged motorists to find other routes as the road was expected to be closed for some time between Glossop and Bamford. An air ambulance also reportedly landed nearby A statement shared by Derbyshire Constabulary said: "The A57 Snake Pass between Glossop and Bamford has been closed due to a collision. Please avoid the area and find an alternative route in the meantime. Thank you." No further details about the nature of the collision have been confirmed. Derbyshire Police have been approached for a statement by the M.E.N

Longsight Music is my life, and it was stolen from me on a Longsight backstreet Finding the instruments is Conor's 'number one priority' News Ramazani Mwamba News reporter 19:32, 07 Jul 2026 View 3 Images Conor Parler-Delvis A music graduate is on desperate hunt to find two saxophones that were stolen from him in Longsight Conor Parler-Delvis was in the process of moving out and had loaded his car with his belongings, only to find them missing on the morning of June 30 Several items were stolen, but the most important were an alto and tenor saxophone which is a family heirloom. Conor says police were called, and while he was able to retrieve some items, the case has since been closed He is still without two of his most prized possessions, however, and now the 22-year-old has been left with no choice but to raise awareness about the stolen saxophones himself A recent graduate at the Royal Northern College of Music that has played gigs in popular spots like Matt and Phreds Jazz Club and Band on the Wall, the instruments are of great importance to Conor who is pursuing a career as a performing musician

Rochdale Heywood residents left suffering vile stench and floors covered in faeces One resident said: "We feel like we are being ignored." Comments News Amy Walker Court reporter 19:22, 07 Jul 2026 View 4 Images The leak in the communal bin shed (Imag Residents were left with a vile stench and faeces on the floors for days after drainage problems said to be caused by nappies and baby wipes The building, on Rochdale Road East, Heywood , which was developed by First Choice Homes Oldham , was left with leaks in the underground car park and the communal bin shed Those living in the building, containing 40 apartments, have reported a stench of 'rotten egg' and faeces on the floor. They said that despite numerous calls to the housing association, nothing has been done to address the issue First Choice Homes says the issue has been resolved today (Monday, July 7) after first being reported on Friday, July 3. The housing association said the issue was caused by residents flushing nappies and baby wipes down the toilet A resident of the building, who wished to remain anonymous, said: "On Thursday (July 2) somebody noticed the grounds in the underground car park were overflowing and leaking. It smelt like rotten egg and there was faeces

Courts Driver mowed down and killed teenage boy on stolen e-bike 'in temper', murder trial told Terrence King, 60, denies murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmons-Caines News Andrew Bardsley Court reporter 18:40, 07 Jul 2026 View Image Devon Simmonds-Caine A driver mowed down and killed a teenage boy who was riding a stolen e-bike ‘in temper’, a murder trial has heard Terrence King, 60, denies murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines. Jurors at Minshull Street Crown Court were told that King has admitted manslaughter Prosecutors alleged that King deliberately hit the boy with the car. Opening the prosecution’s case to the jury, Michael Hayton KC told jurors that Devon died after being hit on Rowrah Crescent in Middleton on July 22 last year The car, a Vauxhall Insignia, was being driven by King. Mr Hayton said: “Tragically, people young and old are killed regularly in road traffic accidents every week. Make no mistake, in this instance the Crown’s case is that this is not a tragic road traffic accident, nor even is Mr Hayton alleged that Devon’s death was ‘caused by the deliberate actions of the defendant Terence King who, in temper or outrage’, made a ‘conscious and deliberate decision to drive towards Devon and to steer towards him at speed and to collide with him’

Bury Man rushed to hospital after bike crash that closed road for hours in Bury Brandlesholme Road was cordoned off by police following the crash News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 18:30, 07 Jul 2026 View Image Brandlesholme Road w A man was rushed to hospital following a bike crash that closed a road for hours in Bury on Tuesday (July 7). A stretch of Brandlesholme Road, to the north of the town centre, was cordoned off near to the junction with Woodhill Road following the serious collision Pictures showed a large police presence in attendance, as well as an ambulance on the scene. A cordon was put in place at the busy junction, with a car also pictured within the police tape Greater Manchester Police later confirmed a car had been involved in a collision with a cyclist . The male cyclist was then rushed to hospital where his condition has not been confirmed No arrests have been made in connection with the incident. A white car was also pictured in the middle of the road at the scene and appeared to be damaged The road was closed off shortly after lunchtime while police and other emergency services responded. Traffic officers manned the cordon amid ongoing enquires throughout the afternoon

Courts Jailed, the drink driver who sped through a red light and killed a motorcyclist on East Lancs Road Christopher Almond was heard to shout 'why me?' after he mowed down and killed David Connah News John Scheerhout Crime reporter 18:23, 07 Jul 202 A motorist has been jailed for speeding through a red light while more than twice the alcohol limit, killing a motorcyclist Christopher Almond, 51, who has a history of dangerous driving and who served time for killing his own brother, undertook a stationary line of cars waiting to turn off the East Lancs Road in Salford , crashing his Audi A5 into David Connah's BMW motorbike Mr Connah suffered horrific injuries after being propelled into bushes 80 metres from his motorbike and died in hospital later. The 43-year-old father had been due to marry his long-time partner within months Today (July 7, 2026) Almond, from Little Hulton, was jailed at Manchester Crown Court after he admitting causing death by dangerous driving and drink driving The court heard the defendant had been drinking the night before and was still over the limit when the crash happened at the junction of Old Clough Lane near Worsley at 1.59pm on May 3 this year. He undertook a line of stationary traffic waiting to exit the East Lancs Road, runni

Leigh Ex rugby league star Gareth Hock spared jail over 'disgraceful' attack on a woman Gareth Hock threw stones at the woman, leaving her with an injured eye News John Scheerhout Crime reporter 18:19, 07 Jul 2026 Updated 18:19, 07 Jul 2026 View Image An ex-England rugby league player has been spared jail for attacking a woman after he had lost his temper Gareth Hock, 45, was 'in drink' when he threw stones at his victim, who was said to have been left with an injured eye The former England and Great Britain rugby league player, who had spells with Wigan Warriors, Leigh Centurions and Salford Red Devils, showed no reaction when Judge Bernadette Baxter revealed she would not be sending him to prison The woman he assaulted said her eye was left 'fully black and blue' following the incident, prosecutor Callum Stevens told Minshull Street Crown Court on Tuesday afternoon (July 7, 2026). He argued the stones he threw at the woman were a 'weapon equivalent', revealing that the de Hock's barrister Vanessa Thomson KC urged the judge to pass a 'proportionate' sentence

Rochdale Reports of disturbance before 'woman with knife' arrested in Rochdale It followed speculation about the incident on social media News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 18:00, 07 Jul 2026 View Image Police were called to an inc There were reports of a disturbance in Rochdale before a 'woman with a knife' was arrested. Police were called to an incident on Sandy Lane in the Spotland area on Monday afternoon (July 6) A woman, aged in her 50s, was later arrested on suspicion of possession of a bladed article and taken into custody. She was later bailed pending further investigations Posts about the incident were shared on social media also claiming that a local school had been put on 'lockdown' and wrongly suggesting there had been a stabbing. It is understood the incident was mental health related A spokesperson for Greater Manchester Police said: "We are aware of concerns on social media regarding an incident in Rochdale "Officers quickly responded to reports of a disturbance around Sandy Lane, in the Spotland area of Rochdale yesterday (6 July 2026) at 2.53pm. A woman in her 50s was arrested for possession of a bladed article and has been bailed pending further investigation

Greater Manchester Police 'I'm devastated': Trust will not be prosecuted over tragic death of teenager at mental health unit "I'm devastated but I can't do anything about it" News Neal Keeling Chief reporter 17:49, 07 Jul 2026 Updated 17:49, 07 Jul 20 An NHS Trust will not be prosecuted following an investigation by police into the death of a teenager, the Manchester Evening News understands Nevaeh Owen passed away in March 2024 aged 18 after she used a ligature at a Greater Manchester mental health unit. At the time, she was under the care of Greater Manchester Mental Health NHS Foundation Trust at Oak Ward at Royal Bolton Hospital Greater Manchester Police detectives began investigating in early 2025 to establish if the incident should be treated as a homicide case, and if offences of corporate manslaughter or gross negligence manslaughter may have been committed It is understood the police investigation centred on the actions of senior managers and staff involved in Neveah's care, as well as the emergency response after she was found unresponsive. It is understood the force sought expert opinion via the Crown Prosecution Service's Comple In August 2024 a pre-inquest review at Bolton Coroners' Court heard Nevaeh died six months after being transferred to the ward. The family's legal representatives told the hearing the teenager's mother and father feared that 'if Nevaeh remained on Oak Ward she would die'

Greater Manchester mayoral election 2026 The underdog with big ambitions: Lib Dem Richard Kilpatrick's on social care, housing and trust Richard Kilpatrick knows he's an outsider in the race to become Greater Manchester's next mayor. But the Liberal D It’s an ambitious set of policies. But with the Liberal Democrat candidate currently polling at fifth place in this race, is any of it actually likely to come to fruition? FocalData have the Liberal Democrats polling at just 7.6 per cent of first preference support in this election. So what’s the point of running? Mr Kilpatrick says the country has faced “ten years of absolute chaos since” Britain left the European Union and people “feel divided “The last 10 years has been a decade of division. I think a sensible, liberal-minded, progressive voice is needed now more than ever,” he says. “I don't see that coming from the extremes of the Greens or Reform, and I don't think I see it coming from a continuity candidate in Lab Mr Kilpatrick is one of the more experienced politicians running in this mayoral election. He was elected to Manchester Council to represent the Didsbury West ward in 2018 and has worked alongside Lib Dem veteran John Leech for many years

The Mancunian Way The Mancunian Way: ‘It’s been abandoned and we’ve been abandoned’ PLUS: The flats where pigeons outnumber people News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 17:22, 07 Jul 2026 Updated 17:23, 07 Jul 2026 View 5 Images The empty plot of land in Ords In the middle of Orsdall sits this patch of fenced-off waste land. Originally it was a shopping precinct and a primary school After they were both demolished around 30 years ago, it became a kind of unplanned, rough-round-the-edges beauty spot. Now it looks, in the words of one disgruntled neighbour, ‘like a nuclear holocaust’ But this wasn’t the plan. It should be home to hundreds of people living on tree-lined streets on a brand new eco-friendly estate And now questions being asked about what’s behind the delays in the building of more than 270 homes on the land off Robert Hall Street in a scheme being managed by the council's development company Dérive.The plans, which include 100 much-needed affordable houses in what is still View 5 Images An artist's impression of how the estate will look

Trafford Decision due on 251-home plan for Stretford Some feel the scheme is 'excessive' and 'unjustified' Comments News Hannah Richardson 17:20, 07 Jul 2026 View Image CGI of apartment block planned on the site of Stretford Mall (Image: Bruntwood) Trafford council is set to rule on a plan that could see 251 apartments built in Stretford The block, ranging from four to 10 storeys in height, would be located next to the multi-storey carpark in the town centre on land currently occupied by the former Stretford Mall. It would be the first phase of housing to be approved as part of the wider regeneration of the area Some 120 one-bed and 115-two bed flats would be delivered, along with 16 three-bed duplexes – a slight increase on the 248 homes originally planned for the site. Future residents would also have access to a central courtyard The ground floor of the building would further feature two retail units, with tenants still to be announced for these. A public park is additionally proposed as part of the scheme, which would sit to the east of the block. It would offer formal play space, open lawns, planted gar A handful of objections were raised by local residents over the plans. These highlight concerns over parking provision, ‘excessive’ and ‘unjustified’ height of the development and the ‘unsafe’ passage through the site

Wigan Block of 22 affordable homes completed at Ashton-in-Makerfield’s Pit Pony pub site The site, which had stood vacant since 2022 will see homes let for 'social rent' Comments News Chris Gee Local democracy reporter 17:16, 07 Jul 2026 View 2 Images The site of a former pub has been transformed into 22 affordable homes Developer Watson has handed over the block of flats to affordable housing group Jigsaw Homes, on the site of the former Pit Pony Inn, Ashton-in-Makerfield. Units at the housing complex will be offered for social rent The homes consist of three two-bedroom apartments and 19 one-bedroom apartments Watson said it had ‘transformed a long-vacant brownfield site into a sustainable residential development that delivers 22 affordable homes and lasting social value for the local community’ The Pit Pony site, on Low Bank Road, had stood vacant since 2022

Cheshire Heartbreak as 'fantastic' young man, 18, dies in horror crash George Gaffney, who had recently completed his A levels, was the front seat passenger in a grey Vauxhall Corsa which ploughed into a tree News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking N Tributes have been paid to a 'fantastic' young man who tragically died in a crash when the car he was in smashed into a tree in Cheshire Police are appealing for witnesses and video footage after the fatal smash in Allostock, near Northwich on Sunday (July 5). At around 9.40pm, emergency crews raced to the incident on Holmes Chapel Road Officers attended and cordoned off the road along with fire crews and paramedics. A grey Vauxhall Corsa had collided with a tree. The front seat passenger, now identified as George Gaffney, died at the scene from catastrophic injuries Meanwhile, the driver of the car, an 18-year-old man from Davenham, also sustained serious injuries. He was taken to the Royal Stoke Hospital where his condition is currently described as critical. And a rear seat passenger, a 17-year-old boy from Northwich, was also injured Following his death, George’s family issued a tribute and said: "It is with great difficulty and sadness that we say goodbye to our fantastic son George

Oldham Murder investigation launched two years after 'loving husband and dad' attacked during Oldham night out Father-of-four Ramon Logan was left in a critical condition after going out to celebrate the Euros final in 2024. Now he has tragically pass His attacker, Sebastian Jones, was later jailed following the violent assault in Oldham town centre. Jones, 38, launched into the assault at around 4am on July 15 2024. Footage captured the moment Jones left a nightclub, before heading towards Ramon and punching him The force from the attack caused the victim to fall to the ground, where Jones continued to assault him. Members of the public called police out to the scene on Yorkshire Street, who discovered the Ramon on the floor with serious injuries to his head. He was rushed to hospital in Jones evaded police for a week and a manhunt was launched, before he was arrested in Bury and charged with S18 assault. Following a three-day trial, Jones, of Dickens Street in Oldham was found guilty and jailed for seven years View 4 Images Sebastian Jones was jailed over the assault (Image: GMP) However, it has been confirmed that Ramon, known fondly by loved ones as 'Ray', died on June 30 this year aged 42. An investigation into murder has been reopened by Greater Manchester Police, but no arrests have yet been made

Courts Moment 97-year-old is hugged and kissed by women in Manchester before their disgusting ruse became clear They have now been jailed News Chris Slater Senior Reporter 06:01, 08 Jul 2026 Updated 07:19, 08 Jul 2026 Three women jailed for string of The vulnerable 97 year-old victim, who uses a walking aid, was targeted as she waited for her husband to pick her up on Dale Street in Manchester city centre on January 10 this year She was approached by prolific thieves Geta Schian, 33, and Sefora Mazarache, 27, who began to hold her hand, hug her and kiss her However it was all a ruse to distract her as they stole a jade antique bracelet off her hand. Manchester City Centre Plan for new floating walkways in Manchester making ‘significant’ progress Once just an idea, steps are being taken to turn an exciting vision into reality. Comments News Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 05:57, 08 Jul 2026 Punters eating out in Manchester city centre could one day head home on a floating walkway along the River Irwell It might sound like a vision of the distant future, but bosses leading a project to build a 100-mile network of brightly-coloured walking and cycling routes across the city say the plan is making real progress The proposal, known as CyanLines, could completely change how residents and commuters trek around Manchester “We are well underway with agreeing and co-designing the first phase of CyanLines routes with our partners, including building our investment case for them,” said Tom Bloxham, chair of property developer Urban Splash and a co-founder of CyanLines The next step, Bloxham explained, is to ‘lobby for national and international funding’ to get things really moving. A vision for the scheme was first announced in September . It aims to spend £100m building a network of floating walking routes based around the city’s waterways

Stockport Stockport man accuses council of stabbing him in the back like Julius Caesar The local authority however argues the purchase was necessary to move a £145m development forward. Comments News Edward Barnes Local Democracy Reporter 20:00, 07 Ju A Stockport man has accused his own council of ‘stabbing a knife in the back’ after they bought his property against his will. The local authority however argues the purchase was necessary to move a £145m development forward In June 2025, Stockport Council’s cabinet made a decision to use compulsory purchase order powers to buy up 72 and 74 Wellington Road South in the town, owned by local solicitor Kieran Henry. These powers allow councils and other public organisations to buy up land or property wi This was being done to move forward the Stockport Exchange scheme , a huge employment development , that the local authority said is ‘critical to the continued growth of the town’. The development includes plans for new offices, homes, and retail as well as leisure facilities A report before councillors said the council had bought all the land in the area up except for the site on Wellington Road South, which ‘acts as a barrier between the town centre and the railway station’ and was holding the entire £145m scheme back. The council want to demolish t The report said: “Despite the council’s sustained efforts to engage and negotiate with the landowner over a prolonged period of over 10 years, voluntary agreement for the acquisition of the site has not been possible.”

Greater Manchester Police 'I'm devastated': Trust will not be prosecuted over tragic death of teenager at mental health unit "I'm devastated but I can't do anything about it" News Neal Keeling Chief reporter 17:49, 07 Jul 2026 Updated 17:49, 07 Jul 20 An NHS Trust will not be prosecuted following an investigation by police into the death of a teenager, the Manchester Evening News understands Nevaeh Owen passed away in March 2024 aged 18 after she used a ligature at a Greater Manchester mental health unit. At the time, she was under the care of Greater Manchester Mental Health NHS Foundation Trust at Oak Ward at Royal Bolton Hospital Greater Manchester Police detectives began investigating in early 2025 to establish if the incident should be treated as a homicide case, and if offences of corporate manslaughter or gross negligence manslaughter may have been committed It is understood the police investigation centred on the actions of senior managers and staff involved in Neveah's care, as well as the emergency response after she was found unresponsive. It is understood the force sought expert opinion via the Crown Prosecution Service's Comple In August 2024 a pre-inquest review at Bolton Coroners' Court heard Nevaeh died six months after being transferred to the ward. The family's legal representatives told the hearing the teenager's mother and father feared that 'if Nevaeh remained on Oak Ward she would die'

Greater Manchester mayoral election 2026 The underdog with big ambitions: Lib Dem Richard Kilpatrick's on social care, housing and trust Richard Kilpatrick knows he's an outsider in the race to become Greater Manchester's next mayor. But the Liberal D It’s an ambitious set of policies. But with the Liberal Democrat candidate currently polling at fifth place in this race, is any of it actually likely to come to fruition? FocalData have the Liberal Democrats polling at just 7.6 per cent of first preference support in this election. So what’s the point of running? Mr Kilpatrick says the country has faced “ten years of absolute chaos since” Britain left the European Union and people “feel divided “The last 10 years has been a decade of division. I think a sensible, liberal-minded, progressive voice is needed now more than ever,” he says. “I don't see that coming from the extremes of the Greens or Reform, and I don't think I see it coming from a continuity candidate in Lab Mr Kilpatrick is one of the more experienced politicians running in this mayoral election. He was elected to Manchester Council to represent the Didsbury West ward in 2018 and has worked alongside Lib Dem veteran John Leech for many years

Trafford Decision due on 251-home plan for Stretford Some feel the scheme is 'excessive' and 'unjustified' Comments News Hannah Richardson 17:20, 07 Jul 2026 View Image CGI of apartment block planned on the site of Stretford Mall (Image: Bruntwood) Trafford council is set to rule on a plan that could see 251 apartments built in Stretford The block, ranging from four to 10 storeys in height, would be located next to the multi-storey carpark in the town centre on land currently occupied by the former Stretford Mall. It would be the first phase of housing to be approved as part of the wider regeneration of the area Some 120 one-bed and 115-two bed flats would be delivered, along with 16 three-bed duplexes – a slight increase on the 248 homes originally planned for the site. Future residents would also have access to a central courtyard The ground floor of the building would further feature two retail units, with tenants still to be announced for these. A public park is additionally proposed as part of the scheme, which would sit to the east of the block. It would offer formal play space, open lawns, planted gar A handful of objections were raised by local residents over the plans. These highlight concerns over parking provision, ‘excessive’ and ‘unjustified’ height of the development and the ‘unsafe’ passage through the site

Mr Hayton said: “Tragically, people young and old are killed regularly in road traffic accidents every week. Make no mistake, in this instance the Crown’s case is that this is not a tragic road traffic accident, nor even is Mr Hayton alleged that Devon’s death was ‘caused by the deliberate actions of the defendant Terence King who, in temper or outrage’, made a ‘conscious and deliberate decision to drive towards Devon and to steer towards him at speed and to collide with him’

practical_angle: Проверить, действует ли предупреждение в вашем районе и нужна ли смена планов на день.
raw_evidence: British "mules" caught smuggling cannabis from Thailand face jail and a charge for every kilo they are carrying under new tough measures to combat a drug trafficking "explosion" The National Crime Agency (NCA) warning came after a BBC North West investigation heard how gangs were making huge profits sending couriers from Bangkok to the UK with loads in suitcases destined for the streets of the cities and towns of the north of England The agency is joining Thai Authorities and the Home Office to combat the surge Anyone caught trying to fly the drug out of Thailand will have to pay Thai Customs 30,000 Baht – the equivalent to around £680 per kilogram of cannabis – to leave the country The number of smuggling attempts from Thailand has continued to rise sharply – despite efforts to tighten relaxed laws there around cultivation – as dealers attempt to cash in on huge demand for super-strength weed Last year, 976 air passengers were caught arriving in the UK with cannabis, a massive increase from the 142 arrested in 2023

raw_evidence: An aspiring musician has spoken about the "horrible" moment when he realised the saxophone given to him by his late grandfather had been stolen Conor Parker-Delves spent his last night at his university house in Longsight, Manchester, on 29 June, after studying saxophone performance at Royal Northern College of Music for four years But when he stepped outside the following morning, the car packed with his possessions had been opened, and two saxophones worth a total of £12,000 had been taken Parker-Delves said one of the items stolen was key to his family's memory of his grandad, and that raising the money to replace the instruments was "nearly impossible" The gold tenor Selma Mark VI first belonged to Parker-Delves' grandfather, Frank Parker, who was "massively obsessed with saxophone and jazz music" "He passed away when I was very young and so I've been incredibly lucky to play on it," Parker-Delves told BBC Radio Manchester

raw_evidence: The home secretary will change the law so the freed ringleader of a Rochdale grooming gang can be deported, the BBC has been told As first reported by the Telegraph, Shabana Mahmood is expected to set out on Monday how she plans to amend the 1971 Immigration Act which currently stops Shabir Ahmed being removed from the UK Ahmed, 73, was jailed for 22 years in August 2012 for a number of child sexual offences including rape. He was released on licence last week Known to his victims as "Daddy", Ahmed had dual British-Pakistani citizenship but was stripped of the former following his 2012 conviction It is not known how long it would take to change the law but one government source suggested it could potentially be up to a year At the time of his release, his victims were told he could not be deported to Pakistan due to the 55-year-old Immigration Act, which bars the removal of any Commonwealth citizen who arrived in the UK before 1973 and had been in the country for five years

A couple who went to see the American rapper Pitbull perform at a festival on their wedding day say their 15-hour celebrations were "a bit mad" Kym Sutcliffe and Tyler Scarisbrick, from St Helens, got married at St Margaret's and All Hallows Church in Warrington on Sunday After a quick pit stop at a local pub, they saw Pitbull aka Mr Worldwide headline the Lytham Festival's final day before rounding everything off by finding a bar to watch England beat Mexico in the last 16 of the World Cup Pitbull fans are known for emulating the star's bald appearance at his gigs. Kym told BBC Radio Lancashire : "I had my wedding dress on, and my bald cap, and my goatee, I must have looked a right picture!" The couple had only been engaged for about six weeks and brought forward their wedding plans in the hope that an ill family member could attend Unfortunately, their relative's health took a turn for the worse and they were unable to make the wedding, which Kym said had made the occasion "bittersweet"

A council investigation into England flags and bunting being fixed to lamp-posts in a Cheshire street has been described as "over the top" and "harsh" by its residents People living in Church Road, Northwich, decided to get into the World Cup spirit with St George's flags and the flags of other nations including Scotland - hanging from windows and criss-crossing the street However, a letter sent by Cheshire West and Chester Council said it was investigating the decorations being unlawfully fixed to lamp-posts and asked for information on who was responsible The authority told the BBC flags and bunting fixed to lamp-posts posed an "avoidable hazard" to road users The letter to residents cited legislation including the Anti-Social Behaviour Act 2003 and the Highways Act 1980 Maxine Simeone, who lives on Church Road, said the decorations were a "just a bit of fun" to celebrate the national football team

Manchester New restrictions set to be brought in for seven Manchester schools targeting drivers It aims to reduce traffic outside the Manchester schools Comments News Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 05:54, 09 Jul 2026 View 2 Images A generic pic Cameras could be installed to fine drivers breaking traffic restrictions outside Manchester schools The council is planning to put automatic number plate recognition cameras outside seven designated ‘school streets’ - roads outside schools with restrictions for cars during drop off and pick up times The idea behind school streets is to make it easier for children to get to and from school, without having to wade through major traffic. It’s also a way of encouraging families to do the school run using public transport or active travel such as cycling Other benefits include improving air quality and stopping ‘inconsiderate parking’ around schools Most school streets are enforced through barriers and volunteers, but council bosses hope using cameras will reduce traffic outside schools and make the streets feel safer for families and kids

Salford Much-loved nan left housebound after falling out of taxi tragically dies Linda Chadwick's health spiralled 'downhill' after the incident News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 05:50, 09 Jul 2026 View 2 Images Linda Chadwick die A much-loved nan who was 'loved by everyone' has died years after a serious fall out of a moving taxi left her housebound Linda Chadwick, who lived in Salford her whole life, 'went downhill' after the incident in January 2023. Her granddaughter Kay said the driver of a taxi she was getting in accidentally drove off while her foot was still out of the car, causing her to fall to the ground Linda severely fractured her back in two places and was regularly in hospital for appointments when her health then continued to deteriorate Speaking to the M.E.N., her granddaughter Kay said: "She had just been going shopping when she fell out of the taxi. The driver set off too early and she fell over onto the ground and injured her back "Since then her health just went downhill. She became really weak and had balance issues and wasn't herself after that."

Deansgate Manchester will soon have a new park on the forgotten edge of the city centre The land next to the River Irwell is set to change from industrial to greenery Comments News George Lythgoe Local Democracy Reporter 05:49, 09 Jul 2026 View 3 Imag The forgotten industrial edge of Deansgate will be brought back to life as a new city centre park Industrial units and warehouses currently make up the land wedged between Castlefield and Trinity Way. Currently, the area makes people feel like they’ve ‘been ejected from the city centre’, according to one town hall boss Manchester council are now pushing ahead with plans for a new city centre park where the River Irwell meets the River Medlock – hoping to replicate the success they had with Mayfield Park It includes creating the ‘landmark’ park and four new skyscrapers. The ground floors of the buildings could also be opened up for retail or ‘residential amenity’ spaces Also mooted are improved public spaces; enhanced walking and cycling connections; and significant new green infrastructure centred around the River Medlock. The plans include the viaduct arches as well, which have been earmarked for retail and hospitality units

Stockport Dad who collapsed on holiday given devastating news as he's left with nowhere to turn Keith Wardle has hit out at bosses at Stepping Hill hospital in Stockport Comments News Chris Slater Senior Reporter 05:46, 09 Jul 2026 Updated 07:15, 09 J A dad who had to learn to walk again after he collapsed with a rare condition whilst on holiday has been left with nowhere to turn to after hospital bosses announced they were closing a specialist swimming pool which has been as ‘lifeline’ during his rehab Previously fit and active Keith Wardle, 77, from Stockport , was visiting his daughter who lives in Boston in the US in October 2022 when he suddenly fell ill He was rushed to hospital where, following scans, he was diagnosed with Guillain-Barré syndrome, a rare neurological condition that affects people’s senses and movements Dad-of-one and grandad Keith, from Hazel Grove , was ventilated and was unconscious for around a month before he was eventually flown back to the UK by air ambulance After landing at Manchester Airport , he was taken to Stepping Hill Hospital where he spent around a fortnight in the intensive care unit (ICU)

Greater Manchester mayoral election 2026 Watch the Greater Manchester Mayoral Hustings in full Candidates set out their vision for the region’s future Comments News Josh Pennington Content editor 20:09, 08 Jul 2026 View Image Editor of the Manchester The Greater Manchester Mayoral Hustings saw candidates set out their vision for the region’s future as they went head-to-head in their first major public event together Criticisms were aimed at Labour ’s record as the remaining parties argued the boroughs have been left behind and future endeavours need to see them brought onto a more level footing with Manchester. Orbital bus routes, house-building , the reinvigoration of town centres and tax f Moments of tension between the candidates also occurred as they tackled questions on immigration and their stance on trans rights. Green candidate Geraldine Coggins and Reform’s Sian Astley also clashed over the national controversies around Nigel Farage’s donations Questions were put forward by audience members - a selection of residents, students, business people and charity leads from across all of the region’s boroughs. They made their judgements on the answers clear with applause and, in a number of cases, boos echoing around the Cheeth Sian Astley, Reform UK ; Geraldine Coggins, Green; Bev Craig, Labour; Phil Eckersley, Conservative ; Richard Kilpatrick, Liberal Democrat ; and Marlon West, Restore all attended the hustings. Independent candidate Marcus Farmer is also standing in the election

Within Chetham's ancient walls, Greater Manchester asked for honesty Chetham’s has witnessed centuries of upheaval, debate and reinvention. On Wednesday, as six contenders for the Greater Manchester mayoralty took the stage, the audience delivered a s News Rob Williams Features and Perspectives Editor 20:02, 08 Jul 2026 Updated 06:08, 09 Jul 2026 Tucked away behind the bustle of Cathedral Gardens and the modern skyline of Manchester city centre , the ancient buildings of the Chetham School of Music have stood in the heart of t Their sandstone walls have witnessed plague and prosperity, civil war and industrial revolution, the rise of Manchester as the world's first industrial city and its reinvention as one of Europe's fastest-growing urban regions. Every generation has left its mark here Founded in 1421 as a college for priests before becoming Chetham's Hospital and later one of the world's leading specialist music schools, the site represents something uniquely Mancunian: a place where history and modernity exist comfortably side by side. The medieval courtyard There can be few more fitting places to stage a political debate than Chetham's School of Music. Marx and Engels fashioned their plans to bring down global capitalism here Some different ideas have become popular since

Manchester Evening News mayoral hustings player ratings as Craig stands firm and Coggins shines Six mayoral hopefuls faced a packed hall, tough questions and a strict 60-second time limit as they set out their vision for Greater Manchester. From confi Comments News Rob Williams Features and Perspectives Editor 19:56, 08 Jul 2026 Updated 06:43, 09 Jul 2026 It takes a special kind of bravery to sit on a stage in front of a packed room and attempt to explain how you would solve Greater Manchester's housing crisis, fix public tran Six candidates signed up for exactly that challenge on Wednesday afternoon as the Manchester Evening News hosted its mayoral hustings in the magnificent surroundings of the Carole Nash Hall at Chetham's School of Music As always, these ratings are not about whose politics we agree with. They're about who worked the room, who looked comfortable under pressure, who landed their arguments, who survived the toughest questions and who left wishing for just ten more seconds. Here's how we saw it Coggins was a confident voice on the panel and was clearly popular with the audience and at ease. Her framing of the debate was consistent throughout. Labour has let down Greater Manchester overall while building up the city centre with economic deals that have not benefited the Strongest moment: Tackling Reform. Her pointed criticism over the ‘crypto funding’ of Reform and Farage was her most impactful moment and drew lots of applause. Was the only person on the panel to mention Reform’s recent difficulties

Wigan Meet the Wigan radiologist who lived an incredible double life for 18 years He rose to the pinnacle of two very different fields - fulfilling a boyhood dream in the process Comments News Chris Slater Senior Reporter 19:24, 08 Jul 2026 View 4 Ima He is one of the most esteemed medics in his field. But for almost two decades, Dr Subhasis Basu lived a remarkable double life The Consultant Musculoskeletal Radiologist at Wrightinton Hospital in Wigan is also a tennis fanatic Also a fellowship-trained Sports Medicine Radiologist, Dr Basu, who began playing tennis aged six, then channelled his love of the sport into officiating During a 25-year umpiring career, he rose to the pinnacle of the profession, walking onto the world’s most famous courts alongside tennis royalty He has served as a line umpire at a number of grand slam tournaments. That includes 18 Wimbledon championships until his remarkable run in SW19 came to an end in 2024

Radcliffe 'He took advantage of them and they've carried it with them since' Kenneth Goodman, 76, was investigated by police after two victims came forward News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 19:16, 08 Jul 2026 View Image Kenneth Go A pensioner who took advantage of two girls before coercing them and sexually abusing them has been jailed decades later. Kenneth Goodman, 76, was investigated by police following a number of reports against him from two victims Greater Manchester Police said one victim was coerced into sexual activity and reported that he made inappropriate comments towards her Investigations then led police to uncover a second victim, who reported how she was sexually abused by Goodman, GMP said Following his arrest in 2019, Goodman denied the offences, but a jury found him guilty at a trial in March earlier this year Speaking in court, one of his victims said: "I have waited a very long time for this day. The sexual abuse you exposed me to will stay with me for the rest of my life. This is both the recollections of the acts and the subsequent impact they have taken on me

Courts Man who performed sex act next to woman with pram at train station 'couldn't explain why he did it' Michael Wainwright exposed himself at a train station in Hebden Bridge News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 19:04, 08 Jul 2026 A man who performed a sex act next to a woman with a pram at a train station said he 'couldn't explain why' he did it. Michael Wainwright exposed himself at the station in Hebden Bridge on February 22 British Transport Police said that at around midday, a woman approached rail staff to report that Wainwright was masturbating next to another woman who was with a pram The member of staff looked at the live CCTV feed and then went out onto the platform and saw Wainwright had exposed himself. He immediately called the police who responded to the scene The 74-year-old was arrested and taken into custody. It was then that British Transport Police said he admitted the crime and said that although he knew it was wrong, he 'couldn't explain why he had done it' Wainwright, of Ashenhurst Road in Calderdale, pleaded guilty to exposure in April this year and was sentenced at Bradford Crown Court on June 25 to eight months' imprisonment and handed a 10-year Sexual Harm Prevention Order

Burnage Boy, 16, arrested on suspicion of murder after woman dies at house in south Manchester It's understood the victim was related to the suspect News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 18:36, 08 Jul 2026 View Image Police attend the A 16-year-old boy has been arrested on suspicion of murder after a woman died at a house in south Manchester on Wednesday morning (July 8) Officers from Greater Manchester Police , along with paramedics, were called to the scene on Ivylea Road in Burnage following reports of an assault at a house at around 8am The victim, a woman in her 60s was tragically pronounced dead at the scene by responders. A murder investigation was launched by detectives and remains ongoing On Wednesday evening, a police car was pictured along the residential street. A witness said there was a major police presence at a house throughout the day amid investigations A teenager, a boy aged 16, remains in custody to be questioned in relation to the incident. The M.E.N understands the victim was related to the suspect

Manchester Manchester city centre will expand and 61,000 homes built under major scheme The council's 'Local Plan' could soon adopted as early as next summer Comments News George Lythgoe Local Democracy Reporter 18:16, 08 Jul 2026 View Image A view of Manchester is destined to expand dramatically over the next two decades through a 15-year vision that has now been approved The council’s ‘local plan’ is effectively a blueprint for the city’s growth between 2022 and 2039. The expansion of the city centre, 61,000 new homes, improved public transport as well as a plethora of new job opportunities are all mooted in the policy The majority of the new homes (42,000) are expected to come to the expanded city centre area The proposed boundary change would include the Great Ducie Street area of Strangeways, part of Victoria North, Ancoats, New Islington, Manchester Science Park and Manchester Metropolitan University Campus. Some of these areas are already seen as part of the city centre, which sho Other parts of the city earmarked for huge upgrades in the local plan include Sportcity in east Manchester, Strangeways, and Victoria North – an area which includes Collyhurst and some surrounding neighbourhoods

Salford 'I’m in a really dark place at the moment, but here I know I'm OK' A 'lifeline' charity organisation is at risk of closing down - despite offering 22 years of support to those struggling with loneliness News Charlotte Hall Local Democracy Repo “We’d be very upset if this goes,” Gail Barrett, 77, told the LDRS at one of the organisation’s coffee mornings at St Aidan’s Church. “There’s nothing else like this, no community spaces. There’s not much around here anymore, no pubs or anything. It really is a lifeline for peopl The charity organisation was previously funded by the Lottery Fund, but after losing out during the bidding process this year, is due to run out of funding before the end of the year. That means all of its activities – which includes the coffee morning, film screenings, and a lin The impact on the area’s local elderly could be ‘devastating’, according to volunteers and visitors “Without this, you’re isolated,” Ms Jones told the LDRS. “This morning I walked out of my house and got a taxi here because I knew staying at home would make my mental health suffer. I’m in a really dark place at the moment, but in here I know I’m okay.” Ms Jones cares for her daughter full-time, and says even though they’re constantly together, the caring responsibilities can leave her feeling cut-off from the outside world

M62 Rush hour chaos as traffic stopped and lanes closed after multi-car crash on M62 Police and traffic officers were called out to the scene News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 17:59, 08 Jul 2026 View 2 Images All traffic was stopp All traffic was stopped with lanes closed following a multi-car crash on the M62 during rush hour on Wednesday (July 8) Police and traffic officers were called out to the scene on the eastbound carriageway between J11 for Birchwood and J12 for the M60 and M602 Traffic cameras showed how all traffic was temporarily held as National Highways warned of long delays of at least half an hour and two miles of congestion following the incident The incident was reported during rush hour at around 5.30pm. Congestion continued to build as recovery was called to the scene and the vehicles were moved from the carriageway View 2 Images There was miles of congestion (Image: Traffic cameras)

Manchester City Centre CCTV appeal after taxi driver assaulted in Manchester city centre attack The taxi driver was the victim of an attack on King Street News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 17:53, 08 Jul 2026 View Image A CCTV appe A CCTV appeal has been issued after a taxi driver was assaulted in an incident in Manchester city centre last month. Police have shared an image as part of their investigations Greater Manchester Police said two men could 'help with the investigation' into reports of a taxi driver being assaulted outside the Rex Hotel, on King Street The incident is reported to have happened between 11.30pm and 11.55pm on June 17. The victim was injured and left with pain to the neck and a swollen nose Enquiries into the incident are ongoing. Anyone with information is being urged to contact police A statement posted by GMP City Centre read: "We would like to speak to these men as we believe they can help with our investigation of assault on a taxi driver outside The Rex Hotel, King Street, Manchester between 11:30 and 11:55pm on 17 June

Courts Murder accused tells police his intention was to 'block stolen e-bike' with car moments before horror crash Terrence King, 60, denies murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmons-Caines News Amy Walker Court reporter 17:34, 08 Jul 2026 View 2 Images Dev A driver accused of murdering a teenage boy who was riding a 'stolen e-bike' told police his intention was to 'block' the vehicle from leaving Terrence King, 60, denies murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines. Jurors at Minshull Street Crown Court were previously told that King has admitted manslaughter It is alleged that King deliberately hit the boy with the car. Prosecuting, Michael Hayton KC told jurors that Devon died after being hit on Rowrah Crescent in Middleton on July 22 last year The car, a Vauxhall Insignia, was being driven by King. Mr Hayton alleged that Devon’s death was ‘caused by the deliberate actions of the defendant who, in temper or outrage’, made a ‘conscious and deliberate decision to drive towards Devon and to steer towards him at speed and t It was said that Devon suffered serious injuries which proved ‘unsurvivable’, and that he was pronounced dead at the scene at 12.20pm that day

Warrington Thugs use crowbar to smash into gaming shop and steal £60k of Pokémon cards The brothers smashed display cabinets and stole high-value cards for their 'criminal enterprise' News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 16:04, 08 Ju CCTV footage has captured the moment two brothers stole more than £62,000 worth of Pokémon cards during a raid at a gaming shop. Shane Johnson, 37, and his younger brother Keith Johnson, 33, used a crowbar to smash their way into Celestial Collectables, in Warrington on April 8 The pair were caught on camera walking towards the specialist store's till area and shattering display cases before putting high-value items like trading cards, slabs, packs, and booster boxes into a bag. The raid lasted over four minutes and left the store 'destroyed' Shop co-owner Chris Grundy, 30, said: "The break-in left me devastated. A lot of work has gone into the shop, and for someone to destroy it was heartbreaking really "It took us a while to get back on our feet afterwards. But hopefully the court result will be a deterrent to anyone else who is thinking about doing the same." View 5 Images Shop co-owner Chris Grundy (Image: Celestial Collectables / SWNS)

Within Chetham's ancient walls, Greater Manchester asked for honesty Chetham’s has witnessed centuries of upheaval, debate and reinvention. On Wednesday, as six contenders for the Greater Manchester mayoralty took the stage, the audience delivered a s News Rob Williams Features and Perspectives Editor 20:02, 08 Jul 2026 Updated 06:08, 09 Jul 2026 Tucked away behind the bustle of Cathedral Gardens and the modern skyline of Manchester city centre , the ancient buildings of the Chetham School of Music have stood in the heart of t Their sandstone walls have witnessed plague and prosperity, civil war and industrial revolution, the rise of Manchester as the world's first industrial city and its reinvention as one of Europe's fastest-growing urban regions. Every generation has left its mark here Founded in 1421 as a college for priests before becoming Chetham's Hospital and later one of the world's leading specialist music schools, the site represents something uniquely Mancunian: a place where history and modernity exist comfortably side by side. The medieval courtyard There can be few more fitting places to stage a political debate than Chetham's School of Music. Marx and Engels fashioned their plans to bring down global capitalism here Some different ideas have become popular since

Manchester Evening News mayoral hustings player ratings as Craig stands firm and Coggins shines Six mayoral hopefuls faced a packed hall, tough questions and a strict 60-second time limit as they set out their vision for Greater Manchester. From confi Comments News Rob Williams Features and Perspectives Editor 19:56, 08 Jul 2026 Updated 06:43, 09 Jul 2026 It takes a special kind of bravery to sit on a stage in front of a packed room and attempt to explain how you would solve Greater Manchester's housing crisis, fix public tran Six candidates signed up for exactly that challenge on Wednesday afternoon as the Manchester Evening News hosted its mayoral hustings in the magnificent surroundings of the Carole Nash Hall at Chetham's School of Music As always, these ratings are not about whose politics we agree with. They're about who worked the room, who looked comfortable under pressure, who landed their arguments, who survived the toughest questions and who left wishing for just ten more seconds. Here's how we saw it Coggins was a confident voice on the panel and was clearly popular with the audience and at ease. Her framing of the debate was consistent throughout. Labour has let down Greater Manchester overall while building up the city centre with economic deals that have not benefited the Strongest moment: Tackling Reform. Her pointed criticism over the ‘crypto funding’ of Reform and Farage was her most impactful moment and drew lots of applause. Was the only person on the panel to mention Reform’s recent difficulties

The last woman to be hanged in the UK has been granted a posthumous conditional pardon by the King, it was announced today Confirming the news, the Government called it 'an act of mercy recognising the historic injustice of the death penalty in this exceptional case' Ruth Ellis, a nightclub manageress, was executed on July 13, 1955, after being convicted of the murder in Hampstead, London, of David Blakely, who she shot in a pub. She was just 28 and a mother-of-two when she was hanged at Holloway Prison The Ministry of Justice said an application for a pardon was brought by four of Ellis's grandchildren The application, added the MoJ, 'presented that her responsibility was profoundly shaped by domestic abuse, trauma and circumstances that were never properly recognised at her trial' Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy, speaking in the House of Commons on Wednesday, said: "I have the honour to say that His Majesty the King has accepted our advice to grant Ruth Ellis a conditional pardon, the last woman to be hanged in the United Kingdom

summary: The scheme omits the Green Belt element proposed in 2022. Credit: via Turley The investor had proposals for a similar project at Atlas Business Park rejected by the city council in 2023 and has made a series of tweaks it hopes will see the refreshed scheme approved Aviva Investor

raw_evidence: The scheme omits the Green Belt element proposed in 2022. Credit: via Turley The investor had proposals for a similar project at Atlas Business Park rejected by the city council in 2023 and has made a series of tweaks it hopes will see the refreshed scheme approved Aviva Investors has toda y unveiled updated plans for a 400,000 sq ft industrial development close to Manchester Airport on a the site of the former Ferranti electronics factory on the corner of Styal Road and Simonsway The original proposal was rejected as it encroached on the Green Belt. Manchester City Council also said Aviva has failed to prove that the five-unit development would not affect the “operational integrity or safety of Manchester Airport” The revised plans see the Green Belt element removed and the scheme nudged westwards to preserve the protected land As a result, the site of the proposed development now incorporates the vacant former Vodafone building, which would be demolished under Aviva’s plans. The plot is also slightly smaller this time around, measuring 19 acres compared to 23 under the original plans

Energy costs have become one of the more awkward line items for businesses across Greater Manchester For offices, warehouses, manufacturers, hospitality venues, retail units and leisure operators, electricity is no longer just a background overhead. It is now a serious commercial variable, affecting margins, pricing, cash flow and long-term planning That is one reason solar power is moving from a “nice to have” sustainability measure to a more practical business investment Across the North West, more companies are looking at whether their roofs, sites and car parks can generate electricity rather than simply buy it from the grid. The argument is no longer only environmental. The timetable also means traders will have the opportunity to enjoy Following the decision for the market to be operated directly by Wigan Council, additional work is now being undertaken to focus on developing an operating model that works for the people at the heart of the market This insight is now feeding into the final layout of the building’s internal design, ensuring the market’s long-standing businesses remain at the heart of one of the borough’s most significant regeneration projects Wigan Market forms the centrepiece of the wider Fettlers development and will bring the market back to the heart of Wigan town centre, creating a vibrant destination where people can shop, eat, meet and spend time together

Andy Burnham Andy Burnham will become Prime Minister in days as nobody challenges him The former Greater Manchester mayor looks almost certain to become the next Labour leader and prime minister Comments News Ashlie Blakey Live and breaking news repor Andy Burnham is on course for a coronation as Labour’s new leader and the next prime minister as he will be the only declared candidate in the contest The Makerfield MP and former Greater Manchester mayor is the only candidate to have announced he is standing in the race ahead of nominations for the contest opening today (Thursday, July 9). Al Carns, thought to be the final remaining potential challenger to Mr Burnham, ruled hi Former armed forces minister Mr Carns said: “I’d hoped a leadership contest would give us the opportunity for a proper debate. But months of internal Labour politics isn’t what the country needs right now "We’ve got to get on with the job. Andy Burnham ’s earned this and he’s got my full backing.” Prospective candidates need the backing of 81 MPs to put themselves forward to replace Sir Keir Starmer, who resigned last month under increasing pressure from his party . In the absence of any other candidates, Mr Burnham will be formally declared Labour leader at a special conf

raw_evidence: With heatwave temperatures soaring, the thought of a sizzling hot plate of fish and chips might not be top of everybody's favourite dinner ideas - but spare a thought for the chippy staff keeping a great British tradition alive and well Here, the BBC asks a range of frazzled fryers across the north-west of England how they are coping as the mercury reaches over 30C (86F) Some have told how they are determined to keep the batter flowing while others have been forced to power down the kitchen and close their doors Others say delivery orders are booming - as more people opt to stay indoors and order takeaways Andrew Crook, president of the National Federation of Fish Friers - the official body representing chippies - said the extreme heat is "horrendous" to work in and is even worse for those cooped up in mobile fish and chip vans "It can be up to 50°C behind the range," he said, even with air conditioning and fans in place

practical_angle: Проверить, есть ли действующие ограничения, предупреждения или влияние на район сегодня.
raw_evidence: A community group that has puts on activities to help tackle loneliness in Greater Manchester for 22 years has said it could face of closure due to funding issues Lower Kersal Young People and Community Group, in Salford, provides regular activities, including film screenings and mini golf, to help the elderly find company and young people access activities they cannot usually afford But after failing to secure a National Lottery funding grant, the charity said it would run out of money by September, the Local Democracy Reporting Service reports "We'd be very upset if this goes," said Gail Barrett, 77, who attended one of the group's coffee mornings at St Aidan's Church "It is a lifeline for people especially those on their own. There's nothing else like this." The group also has allotment sessions, hosts a line dancing class for the elderly, and organises outings and activities such as cooking classes for people of all ages, including people with special educational needs

raw_evidence: Salford Family issues major update with man, 20, left in ICU after horror moped crash on lads' holiday to Greece The loved ones of Bradley Belhomme may now be able to finally bring him home on a specialised flight News James Holt Senior Live and Break The family of a man who was left seriously injured and in a coma after a horror moped crash while on a lads' holiday to Greece have issued a major update Bradley Belhomme, from Salford , suffered catastrophic head injuries and was left fighting for his life after the collision with a coach on the trip to Zante , in Greece last Friday (July 3), his loved ones previously said Bradley had flown out to the party hotspot with a group of friends on July 2. The party had then rented quads to ride on when disaster struck and the 20-year-old was involved in the serious crash while riding with another friend on the bike, who was also put in hospital Bradley was temporarily taken to hospital in Zante, before being moved over to an intensive care unit in a hospital in Attica, in Athens, his family said. Over the last week, doctors have attempted to reduce Bradley's sedation and reliance on a ventilator but this has been unsucc His loved ones say they may now be able to finally bring him home, as early as this weekend, but that he needs a specialised repatriation medical flight costing in excess of £40,000

raw_evidence: Linear Living's One Lord Street has been designed by AEW Architects. Credit: via planning documents A 24-storey tower is coming to the Green Quarter while Levenshulme Cricket Club is set to host 50 affordable houses now that Linear Living and Southway Housing Trust have had their respective planning applications given the green light by city councillors this afternoon Not so lucky was charity Binas Yisachar, which had its application to convert a care home in Crumpsall into a primary school deferred for a site visit Linear Living received the go-ahead from Manchester city councillors to build 251 homes on a small site in the Green Quarter. The homes will comprise four two-bed townhouses, 82 one-bed flats, and 165 two-bed apartments in a tower reaching 24 storeys at its highest point The brownfield site was once home to commercial units, but these have been demolished for some time. In 2016, it had garnered planning permission for a 16-storey residential building that never came to fruition. The plot sits near to the Strangeways and Cambridge Strategic Regene The scheme has an estimated construction cost of £70m, according to a viability appraisal by CBRE. The consultancy added that the gross development value of the project is £82.8m and, as such, Linear Living is unable to provide affordable housing
